output: The Government Stocks will be repaid at par on May 15, 2014, May 24, 2021, February 15, 2027, and September 9, 2035, respectively.

input: Why has the statement FLM 8 been amended by the Reserve Bank of India?
output: The statement FLM 8 has been amended by the Reserve Bank of India in order to get a comprehensive picture of foreign currency transactions handled by full-fledged money changers for the purpose of compilation of Balance of Payments.

input: What are the instructions given by RBI to banks regarding the issuance of demand drafts for Rs. 50,000/- and above?
output: RBI has instructed banks to issue demand drafts for Rs. 50,000/- and above only by debiting the customer's account or against cheques or other instruments, and not against cash payment.

input: What would be the course of action if a UCB's deposits are eroded beyond 25%?
output: A show cause notice will be issued for cancellation of the bank's license.

input: What is the procedure for freezing funds held by designated individuals/entities according to the RBI circular?
output: The procedure for freezing funds held by designated individuals/entities is detailed in paragraph 6 of the circular dated October 29, 2009, which includes taking action as per the UAPA Order issued by the Government.

input: When will the result of the auctions be announced?
output: The result of the auctions will be announced on December 27, 2019 (Friday).

input: What is the purpose of submitting a Statutory Auditor's certificate with the claims?
output: A Statutory Auditor's certificate is required to certify the claims for subvention for the financial year ended March 31 of the corresponding period.

input: When was NASHWAN ABD AL-RAZZAQ ABD AL-BAQI first listed on the Al-Qaida Sanctions List?
output: October 6, 2001.

input: What is the significance of the RBI's decision to allow the cover leg of short-sale transactions outside the NDS-OM platform?
output: The RBI's decision to allow the cover leg of short-sale transactions outside the NDS-OM platform provides more flexibility for banks and primary dealers to cover their short positions in government securities.

input: What is the periodicity of the Quarterly Return on important financial parameters for non-deposit taking NBFCs with assets between Rs.50 crore and Rs.100 crore?
output: This return is to be submitted on a quarterly basis.

input: What is the proposed change in calculation of interest on savings bank accounts?
output: The proposed change is to calculate interest on savings bank accounts on a daily product basis instead of the current monthly basis.

input: What factors must be considered by SC/RCs when valuing assets for buyback by promoters?
output: SC/RCs must consider the current value of the proposed settlement, the net present value of recoveries under alternative modes of resolution, potential changes in the value of the asset over time, and other factors that may affect recoveries.

input: What is the last date for repayment of the first installment by farmers under the Agricultural Debt Waiver and Debt Relief Scheme, 2008?
output: The last date for repayment of the first installment by farmers under the Agricultural Debt Waiver and Debt Relief Scheme, 2008 has been extended from September 30, 2008 to March 31, 2009.

input: What are the two reporting forms that can be filed on the e-Biz platform?
output: The two forms are the Advance Remittance Form (ARF) and the FCGPR Form.

input: How can banks access the complete details of the consolidated list of terrorist individuals/organizations?
output: The complete details of the consolidated list are available on the UN website: http://www.un.org/sc/committees/1267/consolist.shtml.

input: Can the Reserve Bank of India reject bids for the auction of Government of India Dated Securities?
output: Yes, the Reserve Bank of India has the full discretion to accept or reject any or all bids either wholly or partially without assigning any reason.

input: What is the eligibility criteria for Star Trading Houses / Premier Trading Houses (STH/PTH) to import gold under the 20:80 scheme?
output: The STH/PTH should have imported gold prior to the introduction of the 20:80 scheme and should get the required verification done by the Department of Customs at any port where they have imported gold consignment in the past.

input: What is the purpose of the additional allowance for infrastructure sector exposure?
output: The additional allowance is provided to support FIs in financing infrastructure projects, which are essential for the country's development.

input: When will these facilities be available for units in SEZs?
output: These facilities will be available for shipments made on or after April 1, 2003.

input: What is the significance of reporting SHG lending under a separate segment in the priority sector?
output: Reporting SHG lending under a separate segment in the priority sector makes it easier for banks to report their lending to SHGs and/or NGOs for on-lending to SHGs/members of SHGs. It also helps in tracking the progress of SHG lending and meeting the targets set by the government.

input: When will the National Electronic Funds Transfer (NEFT) system be available on a 24x7 basis?
output: The NEFT system will be available on a 24x7 basis from December 2019, as per the Payment System Vision 2021 document.

input: What is the purpose of A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 120 dated June 26, 2013?
output: The purpose of A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 120 is to allow credit enhancement for domestic debt raised through issue of INR bonds/debentures by all borrowers eligible to raise ECB under the automatic route.

input: Which banks does the circular on Change in Bank Rate apply to?
output: The circular is applicable to all banks in India.

input: How has the RBI's stance on accepting the UIDAI letter as an officially valid document for opening bank accounts changed?
output: After further consultations with the Government, the RBI has decided to accept the UIDAI letter as an officially valid document for opening bank accounts without the limitations applicable to 'Small' accounts, as prescribed in the RBI circular RPCD.CO.RRB.AML.BC.No.21/03.05.33(E)/2011-12 dated October 13, 2011.

input: What is the special dispensation granted to banks for SLR securities acquired between September 1, 2020 and March 31, 2021?
output: Banks have been granted a special dispensation of enhanced HTM limit of 22% of NDTL for SLR securities acquired between September 1, 2020 and March 31, 2021, until March 31, 2022.

input: What has been decided in consultation with the Government of India regarding the closure of Residual Transactions for the month of March 2006?
output: The date of closure for Residual Transactions for the month of March 2006 has been fixed as April 15, 2006.

input: Are banks allowed to conduct actual trade transactions through Internet based platforms for dealing in foreign exchange?
output: No, banks are only allowed to report and initiate foreign exchange related transactions through Internet based platforms. Actual trade transactions can only be conducted after verification of physical documents.

input: What is the eligibility criteria for goods, services, machinery, and equipment to be financed under this Line of Credit?
output: The goods, services, machinery, and equipment must be eligible for export under the Foreign Trade Policy of the Government of India and must be agreed to be financed by Exim Bank under this Agreement.

input: Who is eligible to invest in the Sovereign Gold Bonds 2016-17 – Series I?
output: Individuals, trusts, charitable institutions, and universities who are residents of India are eligible to invest in the Sovereign Gold Bonds 2016-17 – Series I.

input: What is the responsibility of banks in regards to customer service for prepaid payment instruments issued to corporates?
output: Banks are responsible for all customer service aspects related to prepaid payment instruments issued to corporates, including facilitating transfer of funds from these instruments to a regular bank account of the concerned employee if requested for.

input: What changes were made in the Foreign Exchange Management (Transfer or Issue of Security by a Person Resident Outside India) Regulations, 2000 through the Fourth Amendment Regulations of 2004?
output: The Fourth Amendment Regulations of 2004 made changes in Regulation 2(vii a) and Regulation 5(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management (Transfer or Issue of Security by a Person Resident Outside India) Regulations, 2000.

input: What is the new CRR that Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks are required to maintain and when does it come into effect?
output: The new CRR for Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks is 6.00 per cent of their NDTL. It comes into effect from the fortnight beginning April 24, 2010.

input: What procedure should RRBs follow in order to comply with the UAPA Order dated August 27, 2009?
output: RRBs should strictly follow the procedure laid down in the UAPA Order dated August 27, 2009 enclosed to the circular dated November 05, 2009 and ensure meticulous compliance to the Order issued by the Government.

input: How will the Government Stocks be issued to successful bidders?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued by credit to Subsidiary General Ledger Account (SGL) or in the form of Stock Certificate.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government of India Dated Securities will be issued?
output: The Government of India Dated Securities will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs. 10,000 and in multiples of Rs. 10,000 thereafter.

input: What is the deadline for submitting bids for the Government Stocks?
output: All bids should be submitted by 12.30 pm on November 8, 2004.

input: Where can holders tender the securities for repayment?
output: Holders can tender the securities for repayment at Public Debt Offices, Treasuries/Sub-Treasuries, or branches of State Bank of India or its Associate Banks.

input: What is the Benchmark Prime Lending Rate (BPLR) and how is it determined?
output: The BPLR is the reference rate for credit limits of over Rs.2 lakh. Each bank's BPLR is determined by taking into account their actual cost of funds, operating expenses, and a minimum margin to cover regulatory requirements and profit margin.

input: What is the maximum tenor of gold loan for import purposes as per the Foreign Trade Policy 2009-14?
output: The maximum tenor of gold loan for import purposes is 270 days, consisting of 90 days for manufacture and export and 180 days for fixing the price and repayment of the loan.

input: What is the recommendation made by the Committee constituted to Review the Facilities for Individuals under the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999?
output: The Committee has recommended that sale proceeds of FDI investments may be permitted to be credited to NRE/FCNR accounts.

input: What is the maximum distance criteria for the operation of a Business Correspondent (BC) for rural, semi-urban and urban areas, as announced in the Annual Policy Statement for 2009-10?
output: The maximum distance criteria for the operation of a Business Correspondent (BC) for rural, semi-urban and urban areas has been increased from 15 kms. to 30 kms. as announced in the Annual Policy Statement for 2009-10.

input: What is the requirement for advance remittance exceeding USD 100,000 or its equivalent?
output: For advance remittance exceeding USD 100,000 or its equivalent, AD Category – I banks are required to obtain an unconditional, irrevocable standby Letter of Credit or a guarantee from an international bank of repute situated outside India.

input: What is the purpose of banks arranging lines of credit from banks abroad or from banks in India for funding export operations?
output: The purpose is to make credit available to exporters at internationally competitive rates.

input: What is the role of the Audit Committee of an authorized CCP?
output: The Audit Committee of an authorized CCP is responsible for reviewing and monitoring the auditor's independence and performance, examining financial statements, and scrutinizing inter-corporate loans and investments.

input: How will the Government Stocks be issued to the successful bidders?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued by credit to Subsidiary General Ledger Account (SGL) of parties maintaining such account with Reserve Bank of India or in the form of Stock Certificate.

input: What actions should banks take in regards to freezing of funds or assets of designated individuals/entities?
output: Banks should follow the procedure laid down in the UAPA Order dated August 27, 2009 and take necessary actions as detailed in paragraph 6 of the circular dated September 17, 2009, including freezing of funds, financial assets, or economic resources held in the form of bank accounts of designated individuals/entities.

input: What is the purpose of the Terms and Conditions section in the Tender Form?
output: The Terms and Conditions section outlines the rules and guidelines for participating in the Reverse Repo auction.

input: What are the repayment dates for the dated securities?
output: The dated securities will be repaid at par on January 28, 2024; June 17, 2033; June 17, 2043; and June 15, 2049 respectively.

input: What is the timeframe for submitting the form FC-TRS for transfer of shares?
output: The form FC-TRS should be submitted to the AD Category-I bank within 60 days from the date of receipt of the consideration.

input: What are the guidelines for authorised persons in terms of risk categorisation and due diligence measures for customers?
output: Authorised persons are required to prepare a profile for each customer based on risk categorisation and apply enhanced due diligence measures for higher risk customers.

input: What is the purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999?
output: The purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 is to regulate foreign exchange transactions in India.

input: What is the deadline for submitting non-competitive bids?
output: Non-competitive bids should be submitted in the prescribed form before 12.30 P.M. on September 8, 2006.

input: Can the acquisition of debt from other SC/RCs extend the period of realization of assets?
output: No, the acquisition of debt from other SC/RCs shall not extend the period of realization of assets beyond eight years from the date of acquisition of the asset by the aggregating SC/RC from the banks/FIs concerned.

input: What are the provisions that co-operative banks are required to disclose in their Balance Sheets?
output: Co-operative banks are required to disclose provisions on NPAs, provisions on NPAs actually made, provisions required to be made in respect of overdue interest, gratuity fund, provident fund, arrears in reconciliation of inter-branch account, and provisions on depreciation in investments.

input: What are the disclosure requirements for banks in relation to the amortization of the additional liability for family pension?
output: Banks are required to make appropriate disclosures of the accounting policy followed in this regard in the ‘Notes to Accounts’ of their financial statements. They must also disclose the amount of unamortized expenditure and the consequential net profit if the unamortized expenditure had been fully recognized in the Profit & Loss Account.

input: What is the deadline for banks to report on the measures initiated for the MSE sector?
output: Banks are required to submit a report on the measures initiated by the end of December 2008.

input: What is the Gold Monetization Scheme (GMS), 2015?
output: The Gold Monetization Scheme (GMS), 2015 is a scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to mobilize the idle gold lying with households and institutions in the country.

input: What is the purpose of RBI's circular regarding opening of NRO accounts by individuals of Bangladesh nationality?
output: The purpose of RBI's circular is to inform all Authorised Dealer banks and Authorised banks about the new guidelines for opening Non-Resident Ordinary Rupee (NRO) accounts by individuals of Bangladesh nationality.

input: Who should be contacted for further clarification on these instructions?
output: In case any point in these instructions requires further clarification, a reference may be made to the undersigned immediately.

input: What is the effective date for transferring the cumulative balances in all accounts to the Depositor Education and Awareness Fund?
output: The effective date for transferring the cumulative balances in all accounts to the Depositor Education and Awareness Fund is May 23, 2014.

input: Can NDS members submit competitive bids in physical form?
output: No, NDS members can only submit competitive bids in electronic form using NDS.

input: Is agency commission payable under the LOC?
output: No, agency commission is not payable under the LOC. However, the exporter may use its own resources or utilize balances in its Exchange Earners’ Foreign Currency Account for payment of commission in free foreign exchange.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction for the sale of '7.27 per cent Government Stock, 2013'?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction for the sale of '7.27 per cent Government Stock, 2013' and can be allotted up to 5% of the notified amount through the Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility.

input: Can a mortgage guarantee company accept public deposits?
output: No, a mortgage guarantee company is not allowed to accept public deposits.

input: What is the purpose of the Government's decision to extend the deadline for repayment of the first installment for "other farmers"?
output: The purpose is to provide more time for "other farmers" to repay their debts and become eligible for debt relief.

input: What are the sections of the Foreign Exchange Management Act under which this circular has been issued?
output: This circular has been issued under Section 10(4) and Section 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999 (42 of 1999).

input: What is the purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign Exchange Derivative Contracts) (Amendment) Regulations, 2010?
output: The purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign Exchange Derivative Contracts) (Amendment) Regulations, 2010 is to amend the existing regulations to allow persons resident in India to enter into currency futures or currency options on a recognized stock exchange for hedging an exposure to risk or otherwise.

input: What is the duration of the extension for the Interest Equalisation Scheme on Pre and Post Shipment Rupee Export Credit?
output: The extension for the Interest Equalisation Scheme on Pre and Post Shipment Rupee Export Credit is for one more year, from April 01, 2020 to March 31, 2021.

input: How can customers provide feedback or lodge complaints about the exchange facilities provided by the RBI?
output: Customers can contact the Officer-in-Charge of the Grievances Redressal Cell in person or write to him for any suggestions or complaints about the exchange facilities. The contact details are provided in the Citizen's Charter on Exchange Facilities.

input: What is the significance of the clause b of sub-Regulation 2 of Regulation 3 and clause c of sub-Regulation 2 of Regulation 5 of the Foreign Exchange Management (Manner of Receipt and Payment) Regulations, 2016?
output: These clauses allow for the settlement of eligible current account transactions, including trade transactions with Sri Lanka, in any permitted currency outside the ACU mechanism until further notice.

input: What is the new requirement for submission of reports on sale of equipment exported on re-import basis?
output: Documentary evidence showing repatriation of entire sale proceeds or hire charges of equipment given on hire basis to other contractors abroad needs to be submitted to the authorised dealer who is monitoring the project.

input: Who will conduct the auctions for the government securities?
output: The Reserve Bank of India at Mumbai will conduct the auctions.

input: What is the definition of "sweat equity shares" as per the amended regulations?
output: "Sweat equity shares" refer to equity shares issued by a company to its directors or employees at a discount or for consideration other than cash, in exchange for their know-how, intellectual property rights, or value additions.

input: Is there any change in the policy regarding foreclosure charges/pre-payment penalty on floating rate term loans for individual borrowers?
output: Yes, there is a change in the policy regarding foreclosure charges/pre-payment penalty on floating rate term loans for individual borrowers. As per RBI's circular, banks are not allowed to charge any foreclosure charges/pre-payment penalties on all floating rate term loans sanctioned to individual borrowers, with immediate effect.

input: Can co-operative banks charge penalties for premature withdrawal of FCNR(B) deposits?
output: Yes, co-operative banks can charge penalties for premature withdrawal of FCNR(B) deposits, subject to certain conditions.

input: How will the Stock be sold and what are the terms and conditions for the auction?
output: The Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner prescribed in the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008. The sale will be subject to the terms and conditions specified in the General Notification as well as the Specific Notification.

input: What types of securities can be accepted as collateral for loans of value more than Rs. 5 lakh?
output: Only Group 1 securities, as specified by SEBI, can be accepted as collateral for loans of value more than Rs. 5 lakh.

input: What are the guidelines for housing loans under the priority sector?
output: The grant of housing loan for the purpose of the priority sector lending targets will be subject to the instructions on “Priority Sector Lending” as amended from time to time.

input: What is the date of the RBI's circular on UCBs' investments in government and other approved securities?
output: January 21, 2009.

input: What is the potential consequence for banks not adhering to the instructions on information sharing?
output: Non-adherence to the instructions may result in serious action by the RBI, including imposition of penalties.

input: What is the responsibility of Designated Authorized Dealer banks in relation to the ECB application?
output: Designated Authorized Dealer banks should ensure compliance with the extant norms while certifying the ECB application both under the automatic and approval routes.

input: What is the instruction given in the note regarding cheques realized on or before March 31, 2008?
output: The note instructs that all cheques/amounts realized on or before March 31, 2008 should be treated as transactions relating to the current financial year and reported during the month of April (up to April 15, 2008).

input: Who is eligible to participate in the repurchase auction for the Government Stock?
output: Banks and financial institutions are eligible to participate in the repurchase auction for the Government Stock.

input: What is the concern raised by the RBI regarding recovery targets and incentives for recovery agents?
output: The RBI is concerned that high recovery targets and incentives may lead to recovery agents using intimidatory and questionable methods for recovering dues.

input: How will the Stock be sold and what is the method of issue?
output: The Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in a price based auction using Multiple price auction method, as prescribed in the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008.

input: Are there any other permissions or approvals required for the implementation of this circular?
output: The circular states that the directions contained in it are without prejudice to any other permissions or approvals required under any other law.

input: What are the types of Government of India Dated Securities being offered for auction?
output: The Government of India is offering to sell (re-issue) 7.17 percent Government Stock 2015, 7.80 percent Government Stock 2020, and 8.26 percent Government Stock 2027.

input: What are the risks associated with outsourcing financial services?
output: The risks associated with outsourcing financial services include strategic risk, reputation risk, compliance risk, operational risk, legal risk, exit strategy risk, counterparty risk, contractual risk, country risk, and concentration and systemic risk.

input: The extant guidelines of Reserve Bank do not prohibit banks from taking over standard accounts from SCs/RCs. Accordingly, in cases where SCs/RCs have successfully implemented a restructuring plan for the stressed assets acquired by them, banks may, at their discretion, with appropriate due diligence, take over such assets after the ‘specified period’ (as defined in terms of extant guidelines on restructuring) provided that the account performed satisfactorily during the ‘specified period’. Banks may frame a board approved policy containing various aspects governing such take over viz., type of assets that may be taken over, due diligence requirements, viability criteria, performance requirement of asset, etc. However, a bank cannot at any point of time take over from SCs/RCs the assets they have themselves earlier sold.10. Banks shall have a Board approved policy on buy-back of financial assets sold to SCs/RCs/other banks/NBFCs/FIs, etc. The policy shall, inter alia, cover the following aspects:Norms and procedure for buy-back of such financial assets;Valuation procedure to be followed to ensure that the realisable value of financial assets is reasonably estimated;Delegation of powers of various functionaries for taking decision on the buy-back of the financial assets; etc.11. In order to enhance transparency in the entire process of buy-back of stressed assets, it is decided as under:At least once in a year, preferably at the beginning of the year, banks shall, with the approval of their Board, identify and list internally the specific financial assets identified for buy-back from SCs/RCs/other banks/NBFCs/FIs, etc.;Prospective sellers need not be restricted to SCs/RCs. Banks may also buy-back the assets from other banks/NBFCs/FIs, etc. who have the necessary capital and expertise in resolving stressed assets. Participation of more sellers will result in better price discovery;In order to attract a wide variety of sellers, the invitation for bids should preferably be publicly solicited so as to enable participation of as many prospective sellers as possible. In such cases, it would be desirable to use e-auction platforms. An open auction process, apart from attracting a larger set of sellers, is expected to result in better price discovery. Banks should lay down a Board approved policy in this regard;Banks must provide adequate time for due diligence by prospective sellers which may vary as per the size of the assets, with a floor of two weeks;Banks should have clear policies with regard to valuation of assets proposed to be bought back. In particular it must be clearly specified as to in which cases internal valuation would be accepted and where external valuation would be needed. However, in case of exposures beyond Rs.50 crore, banks shall obtain two external valuation reports;The cost of valuation exercise shall be borne by the bank, to ensure that the bank's interests are protected;The discount rate used by banks in the valuation exercise shall be spelt out in the policy. This may be either cost of equity or average cost of funds or opportunity cost or some other relevant rate, subject to a floor of the contracted interest rate and penalty, if any.12. Banks shall review the efficacy of their extant policies on buy-back of NPAs, with focus on valuation of stressed assets, and rework their policies by appropriately adopting the above principles.Questions:1. What is the purpose of the RBI circular dated September 1, 2016?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to provide guidelines on the sale of stressed assets by banks to improve their ability to resolve such assets effectively.

input: Can banks discriminate in the matter of interest paid on deposits?
output: No, banks are not allowed to discriminate in the matter of interest paid on deposits, as per the revised Clause (c) of paragraph 22 of the RBI directive.

input: What is the risk weight assigned to all assets covering PPP and post commercial operations date (COD) infrastructure projects for Infrastructure Finance Companies?
output: All assets covering PPP and post commercial operations date (COD) infrastructure projects in existence over a year of commercial operation shall be assigned a risk weight of 50 per cent for Infrastructure Finance Companies.

input: What is the significance of the circular DBOD.No.FMC.BC.153 / 27.01.003 / 93-94 dated September 1, 1993?
output: The circular mentions the need for branch managers to closely monitor cash withdrawals for large amounts.

input: What are the key factors for further easing of monetary policy?
output: The key factors include data confirming continuing disinflationary pressures, sustained high quality fiscal consolidation, and steps to overcome supply constraints and assure availability of key inputs.

input: What are the applicable guidelines and norms for issuances of rupee denominated bonds overseas?
output: The issuances of rupee denominated bonds overseas are subject to all applicable prudential norms and FEMA guidelines.

input: What is the purpose of A.P.(DIR Series) Circular No.6 issued by the Reserve Bank of India?
output: The purpose of A.P.(DIR Series) Circular No.6 is to extend the period of realisation and repatriation of full export value of goods for manufacturer exporters.

input: What action should be taken by pension paying banks if a pensioner fails to submit Life Certificate for more than three years?
output: The pension papers of pensioners who fail to submit Life Certificate for more than three years should be returned to the FA & CAO who issued the PPO for further necessary action.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular on review of statements/returns submitted by banks?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to inform all scheduled commercial banks about the review of statements/returns being submitted to the RBI and the discontinuation of certain statements/returns that have lost their relevance.

input: What are the two certificates that agency banks are required to submit to RBI along with their agency commission claims?
output: The two certificates that agency banks are required to submit are a certificate by the bank official and a certificate by a Chartered Accountant.

input: What is the revised Bank Rate and when does it come into effect?
output: The Bank Rate has been adjusted by 25 basis points from 6.50% to 6.75% with immediate effect, as announced in the Third Bi-Monthly Monetary Policy Statement 2018-19 dated August 1, 2018.

input: Is there any exception to the rule of immediately crediting excess payments back to the government account?
output: Yes, if the excess payment was due to a mistake committed by the bank, then the amount should be credited back to the government account immediately. However, if the bank believes that the mistake was made by the government, they can take up the matter with the government department and do not need to inform RBI.

input: When was the circular released and to whom was it addressed?
output: The circular was released on October 30, 2014 and was addressed to the Chief Executive Officer of all Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks.

input: What is the role of the Central Pension Accounting Office (CPAO) in relation to the acceptance of nomination forms for pensioners?
output: The Central Pension Accounting Office (CPAO) has advised the RBI that due to non-submission or non-acceptance of nomination forms by pensioners, their heirs are facing difficulties in receiving arrears of pension.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction and what is the allotted amount for non-competitive bidders?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction. Non-competitive bidders can bid for up to 5% of the notified amount of the sale.

input: What is the responsibility of NBFCs in regards to the UNSCR 1267(1999)/1989(2011) Committee’s Al Qaida sanctions list?
output: NBFCs are required to update the list of individuals/entities as circulated by Reserve Bank and ensure that no account is held by or linked to any of the entities or individuals included in the list.

input: What are the two categories into which project loans have been divided for the purpose of these guidelines?
output: Project loans have been divided into two categories: Project Loans for infrastructure sector and Project Loans for non-infrastructure sector.

input: When was the Framework for Revitalising Distressed Assets in the Economy issued by the RBI?
output: The Framework for Revitalising Distressed Assets in the Economy was issued on January 30, 2014.

input: What is the Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in the Auctions of Government Securities?
output: The Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in the Auctions of Government Securities allows eligible individuals and institutions to bid for Government Securities up to 5% of the notified amount of sale.

input: Are there any changes to the previous guidelines for relief measures by banks in drought-affected areas?
output: No, the previous guidelines for relief measures by banks in drought-affected areas remain unchanged, except for the one-time waiver of interest on Kharif crop loans.

input: How is the amount of subvention calculated?
output: The amount of subvention is calculated on the amount of export credit from the date of disbursement upto the date of repayment or upto the date beyond which the outstanding export credit becomes overdue.

input: How can UCBs apply for opening off-site ATMs?
output: UCBs can prepare an application with the approval of their Board of Directors and submit it, along with required annexes, to the respective Regional Offices of RBI.

input: - The contract shall be on 10-year notional coupon bearing Government of India security. - The notional coupon shall be 7% per annum with semi-annual compounding. - The contract shall be settled by physical delivery of deliverable grade securities. - Deliverable grade securities' shall comprise GoI securities maturing at least 7.5 years but not more than 15 years from the first day of the delivery month with a minimum total outstanding stock of `10,000 crore. 9. What are the features of the 91-Day T-Bill Futures contract?
output: The features of the 91-Day T-Bill Futures contract are:

input: What is the purpose of the Master Circular on Bank Finance to Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)?
output: The purpose of the Master Circular is to lay down the Reserve Bank of India's regulatory policy regarding financing of NBFCs by banks.

input: What are the qualifications required for a professional director in an urban co-operative bank?
output: A professional director in an urban co-operative bank should have suitable banking experience at middle/senior management level or a professional qualification in the fields of law, accountancy or finance.

input: What is the responsibility of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) and State and Central Co-operative Banks in relation to the Special Investigation Team (SIT)?
output: RRBs and State and Central Co-operative Banks are advised to ensure that all information and documents required by the SIT are made available to them in a timely manner.

input: How much is the penalty for shortages in soiled note remittances and currency chest balances for notes in denomination up to ₹50?
output: The penalty is ₹50 per piece in addition to the loss.

input: Is there any interest charged on the eligible amount for the period between February 29, 2008 and June 30, 2009?
output: No, there is no interest charged on the eligible amount for the period between February 29, 2008 and June 30, 2009. However, normal interest rates may be charged from July 1, 2009 until the date of settlement.

input: How will the Stock be sold and what are the terms and conditions for the sale?
output: The Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner prescribed in the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008. The sale will be subject to the terms and conditions specified in the General Notification as well as the Specific Notification.

input: Are small finance banks required to report credit data to credit information companies?
output: Yes, small finance banks are required to become members of all four credit information companies and report all credit data to them as per current RBI directions.

input: - 7.35% GS 2024 with a notified amount of Rs 2,000 crore - 7.59% GS 2029 with a notified amount of Rs 7,000 crore - 8.24% GS 2033 with a notified amount of Rs 3,000 crore - 8.13% GS 2045 with a notified amount of Rs 3,000 crore 4. What is the auction date for the government securities?
output: The auction date for the government securities is December 04, 2015.

input: What is the policy of disallowing premature withdrawal of term deposits for bulk deposits?
output: Banks have the discretion to disallow premature withdrawal of term deposits in respect of bulk deposits of `1 crore and above of all depositors, including deposits of individuals and HUFs.

input: Who is responsible for giving prior public notice in case of change in control/management of NBFCs?
output: As per the revised guidelines, the responsibility of giving prior public notice lies with the NBFC, as well as the transferor or transferee, or jointly by the parties concerned.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government Stock will be issued?
output: The Government Stock will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs.10,000 (face value) and in multiples thereof.

input: What is the purpose of the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) Scheme and who is it applicable to?
output: The purpose of the DBT Scheme is to facilitate the delivery of social welfare benefits by directly crediting them to the bank accounts of eligible beneficiaries. It is applicable to Scheduled Commercial Banks, Small Finance Banks, and Payments Banks.

input: What is the minimum amount of availment under the export credit refinance facility?
output: The minimum amount of availment under this facility is Rupees one lakh and multiples thereof.

input: What is the decision taken by RBI regarding case-by-case approval for prudential credit exposure limits?
output: RBI has decided to discontinue the practice of giving case-by-case approval for prudential credit exposure limits.

input: What is the reverse repo rate and how has it been adjusted in the latest MPC meeting?
output: The reverse repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India borrows money from commercial banks. In the latest MPC meeting, the reverse repo rate was adjusted to 6.0 per cent.

input: What is the penalty for failure in settlement of repo transactions of G-Sec?
output: Failure in settlement of repo transactions of G-Sec would attract penal provisions as outlined in the circular IDMD.DOD.17/11.01.01(B)/2010-11 dated July 14, 2010.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular on appointment of Chief Risk Officer in Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks?
output: The purpose of the circular is to ensure that all UCBs have appropriate risk management mechanisms in place by appointing a Chief Risk Officer.

input: Under which sections of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 have the directions contained in this circular been issued?
output: The directions contained in this circular have been issued under Sections 10(4) and 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

input: When will the revised all-in-cost ceiling for trade credits come into effect?
output: The revised all-in-cost ceiling for trade credits will come into effect immediately.

input: What actions can be taken by AD Category - I banks if they receive a request for ECB from a corporate under investigation?
output: AD Category - I banks can approve the proposal and inform the concerned agencies, as per the RBI notification. They can also bring the contents of the notification to the notice of their constituents and customers concerned.

input: How should banks view these instructions in relation to improving customer service?
output: These instructions should be viewed as a critical element for bringing about significant improvement in the quality of customer service provided to survivor(s)/nominee(s) of deceased depositors.

input: How long do banks have to put in place the system for monitoring loan proposals and reviewing pending applications?
output: Banks have 30 days from the date of the circular to put in place the system for monitoring loan proposals and reviewing pending applications.

input: Can the Bonds be used as collateral for loans?
output: Yes, the Bonds can be used as collateral for loans. The Loan to Value ratio will be as applicable to ordinary gold loans mandated by the RBI.

input: What is the reason for the temporary revision in the interest rate ceilings on rupee export credit by scheduled commercial banks?
output: The recent developments in the US and its impact on Indian trade have led to a temporary revision in the interest rate ceilings on rupee export credit by scheduled commercial banks.

input: What should be done with notes adjudicated at currency chests?
output: Full value paid notes should be remitted to the Issue Office, while half value paid notes and rejected notes can be remitted separately or sent by registered and insured post.

input: What is the purpose of the Operational Risk section in the Excel Workbook?
output: The purpose of the Operational Risk section is to evaluate the capital charge for operational risk under the New Capital Accord.

input: How often will the system-wide average figure for computing achievement under priority sector lending be notified?
output: The system-wide average figure for computing achievement under priority sector lending will be notified at the beginning of each year.

input: What types of instruments can be issued by the Reserve Bank of India, in consultation with the Government of India, under the issuance calendar?
output: The Reserve Bank of India, in consultation with the Government of India, can issue different types of instruments, including instruments having non-standard maturity and Floating Rate Bonds (FRBs), including CPI linked inflation linked bonds, depending upon the requirement of the Government of India, evolving market conditions and other relevant factors.

input: What is the significance of the Information Technology Vision Document 2011-2017?
output: The Information Technology Vision Document 2011-2017 sets priorities for commercial banks to move towards using technology for areas such as management information system, regulatory reporting, risk management, financial inclusion, and customer relationship management.

input: How will the Government Stocks be issued to successful bidders?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued by credit to Subsidiary General Ledger Account (SGL) or in the form of Stock Certificate.

input: Who should be contacted for any further clarifications or queries regarding the revised procedure for remittance of Government revenues?
output: Any further clarifications or queries regarding the revised procedure for remittance of Government revenues can be addressed to the Deputy General Manager, DGBA.GAD.No.H-11763 / 42.01.011 /2006-07.

input: What is the purpose of the circular issued by RBI regarding the winding up of the State Level Export Promotion Committee (SLEPC)?
output: The circular issued by RBI is regarding the winding up of the State Level Export Promotion Committee (SLEPC) and the constitution of a separate Sub-Committee under State Level Bankers’ Committee (SLBC).

input: What is the procedure for obtaining certified copies of judgments in cases of delay?
output: Banks may follow the procedure of obtaining a signed copy of the minutes of the order certified by the Registrar / Deputy or Assistant Registrar of the High Court duly attested to be true copy by Govt. Counsel for delay in obtaining certified copies of judgments.

input: What is the action advised to UCBs maintaining current account balances with IDBI Bank Limited?
output: UCBs maintaining current account balances with IDBI Bank Limited are advised to intimate the position of their balances as on June 30, 2008 to the respective Regional Offices of Reserve Bank in the prescribed format provided in the notification.

input: What are the consequences of not submitting Annexures "C" and "D" within the stipulated time?
output: Non-submission of Annexures "C" and "D" within the stipulated time may result in penalties under the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.

input: What is the significance of the term 'principal business' in relation to NBFCs?
output: The term 'principal business' has not been defined in law, but the Reserve Bank of India has now provided a description for the purpose of identifying an NBFC. This is important for determining whether a company falls under the category of an NBFC or not.

input: What is the timeline for submission of the Monthly Statement on Gold Monetisation Scheme?
output: The statement should be submitted within 30 days.

input: When and where will the auction be conducted and what is the tenure of the Government Stock?
output: The auction will be conducted by Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 on September 2, 2016. The Government Stock will have a tenure of thirty years commencing from June 22, 2015.

input: What is the existing limit for remittance of initial and recurring expenses for branch offices opened abroad and how has it been revised?
output: The existing limit for remittance of initial and recurring expenses for branch offices opened abroad is two and one percent of average annual sales/income or turnover during last two accounting years of the Indian entity. It has been revised to ten percent and five percent respectively.

input: How should Urban Cooperative Banks respond to the FATF Statement?
output: Urban Cooperative Banks are advised to consider the information contained in the FATF Statement and ensure compliance with the AML/CFT standards and guidelines. The Compliance Officer/Principal Officer of the bank should also acknowledge receipt of the circular to the concerned Regional Office.

input: What are the undertakings that a bidder must agree to when submitting a non-competitive bid for the auction of government securities?
output: The bidder must agree to immediately collect the letter of acceptance from the RBI office and deposit the requisite amount at the RBI office in Mumbai. They must also authorize the RBI to debit their current account for the bid amount. The bidder must also confirm that they have read and will abide by the terms and conditions outlined in the "Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in Auctions of Government Securities". Additionally, the bidder must confirm that they have not submitted any other non-competitive bids for the same clients for the auction.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular prohibiting banks from crediting 'account payee' cheques to third party accounts?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to prevent banks from crediting 'account payee' cheques to accounts of individuals other than the payee named in the cheque.

input: What is the purpose of the amendment made by RBI in Regulation 6 of the Foreign Exchange Management (Remittance of Assets) Regulations, 2000?
output: The amendment made in Regulation 6 is related to the time period for remittance of assets and the purpose is to ensure compliance with the regulations.

input: What is the date of maturity for the 7.06% GS 2046 security being offered for sale?
output: The date of maturity for the 7.06% GS 2046 security is October 10, 2046.

input: Who is responsible for bringing the contents of this circular to the notice of constituents and customers?
output: AD Category - I banks are responsible for bringing the contents of this circular to the notice of their constituents and customers.

input: What is the method of auction used for the sale of Government of India Dated Securities?
output: The sale of Government of India Dated Securities will be done through a price-based auction using the multiple price method.

input: Can securities be issued in physical form from the main SGL account?
output: Yes, delivery in physical form from the main SGL account is permissible at the instance of the investor subsequently.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI's Resolution Framework for COVID-19-related Stress?
output: The purpose of the RBI's Resolution Framework for COVID-19-related Stress is to provide relief to eligible borrowers who have been impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic.

input: What is the repayment schedule for the Government of India Dated Securities?
output: The Government of India Dated Securities will be repaid at par on June 9, 2020; July 28, 2024; September 30, 2030; and December 23, 2043 respectively.

input: What is the purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign Exchange Derivatives Contracts) Regulations, 2000?
output: The purpose of these regulations is to regulate foreign exchange derivative contracts in India.

input: What are the single/group exposure norms for NBFCs?
output: NBFCs are not allowed to lend to a single borrower exceeding 15% of its owned fund, or to any single group of borrowers exceeding 25% of its owned fund. Similar limits apply for investments in shares of another company or group of companies.

input: When will the modifications to the ECB policy come into force?
output: The modifications to the ECB policy will come into force from the date of the circular.

input: What is the penalty for wrong claims of agency commission?
output: Agency banks will be liable to pay penal interest at Bank rate plus 2% for any wrong claims of agency commission settled.

input: What is the reverse repo rate under the LAF and what was the decision taken by the MPC regarding it at the February 5, 2021 meeting?
output: The reverse repo rate under the LAF is the rate at which the central bank borrows money from commercial banks. The MPC decided to keep the reverse repo rate unchanged at 3.35 per cent.

input: What information can be accessed through the "Trade Watch" page on RBI's website?
output: The "Trade Watch" page on RBI's website displays data on ten latest trades concluded/reported on NDS and provides hyperlinks to see trades in a particular security/instrument.

input: What is the purpose of the SHG-Bank Linkage Programme?
output: The SHG-Bank Linkage Programme aims to provide financial support to Self-Help Groups (SHGs) under the National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM) and National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM).

input: What updates have been made to the UNSCR 1267(1999)/ 1989(2011) Committee's Al Qaida Sanctions List?
output: The 11th update dated April 7, 2015 has been added to the sanction list, as per the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), UNP Division. This update can be found on the UN's website and includes a list of individuals and entities linked to Al Qaida.

input: What penalties can be imposed for non-compliance with these guidelines?
output: Non-compliance with these guidelines can attract penalties under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (AACS).

input: When can a banker proceed against the guarantor in case of default by the principal debtor?
output: A banker can proceed against the guarantor in case of default by the principal debtor, even without exhausting the remedies against the principal debtor.

input: Will the condition of 'abinitio' buyers' credit for 15 months continue for future trade credits?
output: Yes, the condition of 'abinitio' buyers' credit for 15 months will continue for future trade credits.

input: Can UCBs engage the services of agents for Doorstep Banking?
output: Yes, UCBs can engage the services of agents for Doorstep Banking, but they must ensure compliance with the guidelines on managing risks in outsourcing of financial services.

input: Is it mandatory to provide PAN while making remittances under the LRS?
output: Yes, it is mandatory to provide PAN while making remittances under the LRS.

input: What is the validity period for the interest rates on rupee export credit indicated in the circular dated October 29, 2007?
output: The validity period for the interest rates on rupee export credit indicated in the circular dated October 29, 2007 is up to October 31, 2008.

input: What information should be included in the BCP kept in the public domain?
output: The BCP kept in the public domain should include information about the general readiness of the bank without any detailed specifics.

input: When will the interest on the Government Stock be paid?
output: Interest at the rate of 8.35 per cent per annum will accrue on the nominal value of the Stock from the date of issue and will be paid half-yearly on May 23 and November 23 of each year until the date of maturity.

input: What is the revised limit for loans to farmers against pledge/hypothecation of agricultural produce under direct and indirect agriculture?
output: The revised limit for loans to farmers against pledge/hypothecation of agricultural produce under direct and indirect agriculture is increased from `25 lakh to `50 lakh.

input: What is the current state of the global economy according to the MPC's assessment?
output: The global economic recovery slackened in Q4 (October-December) of 2020 relative to Q3 (July-September) as several countries battle second waves of COVID-19 infections, including more virulent strains.

input: How should the views of the Regional Director be forwarded to the Central Office?
output: The Regional Office should forward the views of the Regional Director to the Central Office as soon as possible.

input: Will inward transactions of RTGS members also be charged?
output: No, inward transactions of RTGS members will not attract any service charges and will continue to be free.

input: What is the risk weight assigned to assets covering PPP and post COD infrastructure projects in existence over a year of commercial operation according to the Non-Systemically Important Non-Banking Financial (Non-Deposit Accepting or Holding) Companies Prudential Norms (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2015?
output: The risk weight assigned to such assets is 50%.

input: What is the list of circulars consolidated in the Master Circular?
output: The Master Circular consolidates circulars issued by RBI from 1984 to 2011, a list of which is provided in Annex 5.

input: What is the purpose of the amending Directive RPCD.CO.RF.BC.Dir.No.49/07.38.01/2008-09?
output: The purpose of the amending Directive is to revise the interest rates applicable to deposits accepted under the Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks) Scheme.

input: Can scheduled UCBs accept deposits from non-scheduled UCBs for investment purposes?
output: No, scheduled UCBs are not permitted to accept deposits from non-scheduled UCBs for investment purposes.

input: Can overseas investors hedge their exposure in Rupee for Rupee denominated bonds overseas?
output: Yes, overseas investors can hedge their exposure in Rupee through permitted derivative products with AD Category - I banks in India or through branches/subsidiaries of Indian banks abroad or branches of foreign banks with Indian presence on a back to back basis.

input: Who is eligible to make direct investments in equity of companies registered overseas under the amended regulations?
output: Individuals, listed Indian companies, and mutual funds registered in India are eligible to make direct investments in equity of companies registered overseas under the amended regulations.

input: What is the significance of including National Horticulture Board as a permanent member of State Level Bankers' Committees at the all India level?
output: The inclusion of National Horticulture Board as a permanent member of State Level Bankers' Committees at the all India level is significant as it aligns with the government's priority for investment in the horticulture sector and expects banks to cooperate in a proactive manner towards this direction.

input: What has been decided in view of the difficulties expressed by AD Category-I banks?
output: In view of the difficulties expressed by AD Category-I banks, it has been decided to revise certain instructions contained in the Memorandum of Instructions.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular regarding the list of Terrorist Individuals/Organisations under UNSCR 1267(1999) and 1822(2008) on Taliban/Al-Qaida Organisation?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to inform all Scheduled Commercial Banks, Local Area Banks, and All India Financial Institutions about the changes made in the Consolidated List of Individuals and entities linked to Al-Qaida and Taliban by the UN Security Council's 1267 Committee.

input: What has been the recent complaint from customers regarding the KYC process?
output: Customers have complained that banks are seeking irrelevant and unnecessary personal information, invading their privacy.

input: What is the minimum price for which tenders for purchase of Government Stock will be accepted at the auctions?
output: The Reserve Bank of India will determine the minimum price for which tenders will be accepted at the auctions.

input: IDMD, Reserve Bank of India 1. What is the last date for submitting applications for the Sovereign Gold Bonds, 2017-18 – Series II?
output: The last date for submitting applications is July 14, 2017.

input: What is the timeline for closing the Foreign Currency Account?
output: The Foreign Currency Account must be closed immediately or within 30 days from the date of disinvestment from JV/WOS or cessation thereof.

input: up to USD 10 million per financial year 1. What is the maximum amount of foreign currency borrowings allowed for a borrower under the new ECB policy for MFIs and NGOs engaged in micro finance activities?
output: The maximum amount of foreign currency borrowings allowed for a borrower is USD 10 million per financial year.

input: Can AD Category-I banks offset their positions in the ETCD market against the positions in the OTC derivatives market?
output: Yes, AD Category-I banks may net/offset their positions in the ETCD market against the positions in the OTC derivatives market.

input: What is the product emerging in the context of funding long-term infrastructure projects?
output: The product emerging is take out finance, which is a financing arrangement between financial institutions for transferring outstanding loans in their books.

input: What is the purpose of the review of the Medium Term Framework (MTF)?
output: The purpose of the review of the Medium Term Framework (MTF) is to recalibrate the framework to meet the objective of a preference for long-term investors and manage the macro-prudential implications of evolving capital flows.

input: What is the purpose of RBI's circular on unclaimed deposits/inoperative accounts in banks?
output: The purpose of RBI's circular is to provide guidelines for the treatment of certain savings bank accounts that have been classified as dormant/inoperative due to non-operation for over two years.

input: Which type of NBFCs are eligible to raise capital through Perpetual Debt Instruments (PDI)?
output: Systemically Important Non-Deposit taking Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs-ND-SI) with assets size of Rs 100 crore and above are eligible to raise capital through PDI.

input: What is the role of banks and financial institutions in regards to the updated list of individuals and entities linked to Taliban?
output: Banks and financial institutions are required to update their list of individuals and entities as circulated by the RBI and ensure that no account is held by or linked to any of the designated individuals or entities.

input: What is the purpose of the due diligence process for the introduction of a new product in the Master Direction – Reserve Bank of India (Market-makers in OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2021?
output: The purpose of the due diligence process is to assess the various aspects of the new product, such as its objective, targeted clients, risks, pay-off profile, pricing, costs and fees, and measures to mitigate conflicts of interest.

input: When should banks submit the revised quarterly and yearly statements for priority sector lending to the RBI?
output: Banks should submit the revised quarterly and yearly statements for priority sector lending to the RBI within fifteen days and one month, respectively, from the reference date starting from June 2015 for quarterly statement and March 31, 2016 for the yearly one.

input: What changes have been made regarding the deployment of temporary cash surpluses by project/service exporters?
output: Project/service exporters are now allowed to deploy their temporary cash surpluses, generated outside India, in certain instruments/products, subject to monitoring by the AD Category - I bank(s)/Exim Bank/Working Group.

input: Can Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks continue to maintain SLR as per previous circulars?
output: Yes, Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks can continue to maintain SLR as per previous circulars until March 31, 2015.

input: What is the minimum CRAR required for an IFC?
output: The minimum CRAR required for an IFC is 15%, with a minimum Tier I capital of 10%.

input: What is the minimum requirement for an NBFC to register as an NBFC-Factor according to the revised guidelines?
output: According to the revised guidelines, an NBFC must ensure that its financial assets in the factoring business constitute at least 50 per cent of its total assets and its income derived from factoring business is not less than 50 per cent of its gross income in order to register as an NBFC-Factor.

input: What is the meaning of VFT of government securities?
output: VFT of government securities refers to the transfer of securities from one SGL/CSGL account to another SGL/CSGL account without any consideration.

input: What is the role of G. Srinivasan in the RBI circular?
output: G. Srinivasan is the Chief General Manager of the RBI and has signed the circular.

input: What is the deadline for NBFCs to report compliance with KYC guidelines for persons authorized to collect deposits on their behalf?
output: The deadline for NBFCs to report compliance with KYC guidelines for persons authorized to collect deposits on their behalf is December 31, 2005.

input: What is the procedure for submission of the GR form by the exporter to the AD Category-I banks?
output: The exporter must complete the GR form in duplicate and submit both copies to Customs at the registered port of the vessel or any other port approved by the Ministry of Agriculture.

input: What is the projection of growth for 2014-15?
output: The projection of growth for 2014-15 is retained at 5.5 per cent within a range of 5 to 6 per cent around this central estimate. The quarterly growth path may slow mildly in Q2 and Q3 before recovering in Q4.

input: What happens when the RBI revises the interest rate for NRE deposits for scheduled commercial banks?
output: When the RBI revises the interest rate for NRE deposits for scheduled commercial banks, the same rate will automatically apply to RNBCs.

input: Can the bank or Primary Dealer indicate the amount to be credited to their SGL account and CSGL account?
output: Yes, the bank or Primary Dealer can indicate the amounts to be credited to their SGL account and CSGL account at the time of tendering non-competitive bids.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction and what is the deadline for submitting bids?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction and submit their bids on July 17, 2009. Non-competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m. and competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 12.30 p.m.

input: What is the revised limit on disbursal of loans in the NBFC-MFI sector?
output: The revised limit on disbursal of loans in the NBFC-MFI sector is Rs. 60,000 in the first cycle and Rs. 1,00,000 in subsequent cycles.

input: What is the time period within which an NBFC must dispose off any land or building or unquoted shares acquired in satisfaction of its debts?
output: An NBFC must dispose off any land or building or unquoted shares acquired in satisfaction of its debts within a period of three years or within such period as extended by the Bank, from the date of such acquisition.

input: What is the process followed by the RBI before imposing a penalty on a Primary Dealer?
output: The RBI follows a due process of advising the Primary Dealer and seeking their explanation before imposing a penalty, in order to give them a reasonable opportunity to be heard.

input: Does the RBI circular prohibit Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks from conducting legitimate trade and business transactions with countries and jurisdictions mentioned in the updated statements?
output: No, the RBI circular does not prohibit Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks from conducting legitimate trade and business transactions with countries and jurisdictions mentioned in the updated statements. However, they are advised to consider the information contained in the statements and exercise caution in their transactions.

input: What are the actions that can be taken by the Director under the amended Section 13(2) of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act?
output: The Director can take the following actions under the amended Section 13(2) of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act: issue a warning in writing, direct the reporting entity or its designated director to comply with specific instructions, direct the reporting entity or its designated director to send reports at prescribed intervals, or levy a fine of at least ten thousand rupees but not exceeding one lakh rupees for each failure.

input: Who is responsible for ensuring compliance with the security measures mentioned in the circular?
output: The CMD/CEO of the bank is responsible for ensuring compliance with the security measures.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government Stocks will be issued?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs.10,000/- (nominal) and in multiples of ₹ 10,000/- thereafter.

input: Under which sections of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 has this circular been issued?
output: This circular has been issued under Sections 10(4) and 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

input: How often will the Scheme of Incentives and Penalties for bank branches including currency chests be reviewed by RBI?
output: The Scheme of Incentives and Penalties for bank branches including currency chests will be reviewed by RBI from time to time.

input: What is the tenure of the Stock and when will it be repaid?
output: The Stock will be of thirty-year tenure commencing from October 10, 2016 and will be repaid at par on October 10, 2046.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government Stock will be issued?
output: The Government Stock will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs. 10,000 (face value) and in multiples thereof.

input: What is Tier I Capital/Core Capital?
output: Tier I Capital/Core Capital includes paid-up share capital, free reserves, capital reserves, and any surplus in the Profit and Loss Account.

input: What is the minimum amount that RRBs must amortize every year for the enhanced gratuity expenditure?
output: RRBs must amortize a minimum of 1/5th of the total amount involved every year, starting from the financial year ending March 31, 2011.

input: What are the other aspects that NBFCs must comply with when issuing co-branded credit cards?
output: NBFCs must have fair practices code guidelines in place, adhere to Know Your Customer guidelines and provisions of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, and comply with other terms and conditions specified by the RBI.

input: What is the requirement for the concerned state government for StCBs to be eligible for opening new places of business?
output: The concerned state government should have signed the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) in connection with the Government of India's Revival Package for short term rural co-operative credit structure.

input: How should the amounts in inoperative accounts be audited?
output: The amounts in inoperative accounts should be properly audited by the internal auditors or statutory auditors of the bank.

input: What is the purpose of the amending Directive RPCD.CO.RF.BC.Dir.No.80/07.38.01/2006-07?
output: The purpose of the amending Directive is to specify the interest rates applicable to deposits accepted under Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks) Scheme.

input: What are the prudential norms for capital adequacy that primary (urban) co-operative banks (UCBs) must adhere to?
output: The Master Circular on Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy for UCBs outlines the minimum capital requirements and other prudential norms that UCBs must follow to maintain a healthy financial position.

input: Can non-constituents submit bids through NDS for the non-competitive segment?
output: No, non-constituents can only submit physical application forms to the Primary Dealer Office.

input: Can non-scheduled UCBs shift securities from HFT/AFS categories to the HTM category at book value?
output: Yes, non-scheduled UCBs can shift securities from HFT/AFS categories to the HTM category at book value, subject to certain conditions.

input: What is the expected frequency of visits by BCs to allocated villages?
output: Initially, BCs are expected to visit allocated villages at least once a fortnight, but efforts should be made to increase the frequency over time.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction and what is the allotted amount for non-competitive bidders?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction and the Government Stock up to 5% of the notified amount will be allotted to them.

input: What steps should agency banks take to ensure data accuracy in OLTAS?
output: Agency banks should use bulk PAN verification facility from TIN-NSDL site, insist on proof of PAN where necessary, and ensure that the CIN given to the customer is the one uploaded to OLTAS.

input: What is the role of stakeholders in the review of norms for classification of UCBs as FSWM?
output: The stakeholders have played a crucial role in the review of norms for classification of Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) as Financially Sound and Well Managed (FSWM) by submitting representations to RBI and requesting for a revision of the definition of 'Regulatory Comfort'.

input: What is the method of issue for the sale of the Government Stock?
output: The Government Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 using a price based auction method.

input: What is the purpose of the amendment to Non-Banking Financial (Non-Deposit Accepting or Holding) Companies Prudential Norms (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2007?
output: The amendment aims to include perpetual debt instruments issued by Systemically Important Non-Deposit taking Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs-ND-SI) in the definition of Tier I capital.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government of India Dated Securities will be issued?
output: The Government of India Dated Securities will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs. 10,000 (nominal) and in multiples of Rs. 10,000 thereafter.

input: Who should UCBs inform about the receipt of the circular?
output: UCBs should acknowledge receipt of the circular to the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank of India.

input: What is the interest rate on the Stock and when will it be paid?
output: The Stock will have an interest rate of 7.49 per cent per annum and the interest will be paid half yearly on October 16 and April 16. The interest will accrue from the date of last coupon payment, i.e., April 16, 2007 to May 13, 2007.

input: What is the significance of the Deferred Payment Protocols dated April 30, 1981 and December 23, 1985 between Government of India and erstwhile USSR?
output: These protocols determine the terms and conditions for deferred payments between India and the erstwhile USSR, and are still relevant in present times.

input: What is the purpose of rotating partners of audit firms in NBFCs?
output: Rotating partners of audit firms periodically adds value to corporate governance by ensuring a fresh perspective and avoiding conflicts of interest.

input: Can banks or primary dealers submit non-competitive bids on behalf of their constituents?
output: Yes, banks or primary dealers can submit a single consolidated non-competitive bid on behalf of all their constituents.

input: What is the current status of OCBs as recognized investors under the Foreign Exchange Management Regulations?
output: OCBs were derecognized as an eligible class of investor under various routes/schemes under the Foreign Exchange Management Regulations, as per the RBI notification dated September 16, 2003.

input: Who is responsible for issuing suitable instructions to branches and controlling offices?
output: The banks are responsible for issuing suitable instructions to their branches and controlling offices as per the RBI circular.

input: What is the last date for submitting non-competitive bids for the auction?
output: The last date for submitting non-competitive bids for the auction is June 11, 2009, between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m.

input: What is the purpose of the Credit Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income Housing (CRGFTLIH) and when was it established?
output: The CRGFTLIH was established by the Ministry of Housing & Urban Poverty Alleviation, Government of India on June 21, 2012, with the aim of providing credit risk guarantee for low income housing loans.

input: What is the minimum credit rating required for an IDF-NBFC?
output: The minimum credit rating required for an IDF-NBFC is 'A' of CRISIL or equivalent rating issued by other accredited rating agencies such as FITCH, CARE and ICRA.

input: Are there any other changes to the instructions on the sale of securities allotted in primary issues?
output: No, all other instructions on the sale of securities allotted in primary issues on the same day remain unchanged.

input: Where can the complete details of the Al Qaida Sanctions List be found?
output: The complete details of the Al Qaida Sanctions List can be found on the UN website at http://www.un.org/sc/committees/1267/aq_sanctions_list.shtml.

input: Is there any change in the reporting requirements to CRILC for lending institutions?
output: No, the RBI circular does not make any changes to the reporting requirements to CRILC, which will continue to be governed by extant instructions for respective entities.

input: What is the amendment made in paragraph 4 of the Non-Banking Financial Companies Acceptance of Public Deposit (Reserve Bank) Directions, 1998?
output: In paragraph 4, sub-paragraph (4) is substituted with a new one, which specifies the conditions for equipment leasing companies, hire purchase finance companies, loan companies, and investment companies to accept or renew public deposits.

input: How will the CRR reduction impact UCBs?
output: The CRR reduction will result in UCBs having to maintain a lower average Cash Reserve Ratio of 5.00% instead of the previous 5.50%. This will provide them with more liquidity and enable them to lend more to their customers.

input: What is the purpose of the Master Circular on Priority Sector Lending - Credit facilities to Minority Communities issued by RBI?
output: The purpose of the Master Circular is to provide current instructions and guidelines to banks for providing credit facilities to minority communities, as directed by the Government of India.

input: Can banks write off unreconciled debit entries of individual value less than USD 2500 in nostro accounts?
output: Banks may write off unreconciled debit entries of individual value less than USD 2500 in nostro accounts against the provision already held, at their discretion.

input: What is the modification made to the clause (C) of Paragraph 1 (B) of the Scheme for Bidding, Underwriting and Liquidity Support to Primary Dealers-2001-02?
output: The modification made to the clause (C) of Paragraph 1 (B) of the Scheme for Bidding, Underwriting and Liquidity Support to Primary Dealers-2001-02 is that the penalty period for reduction in liquidity support has been reduced from 6 months to 3 months.

input: What are the income recognition and provisioning norms to be followed by lending institutions?
output: Lending institutions should follow income recognition and provisioning norms based on the record of recovery of the loan.

input: Can the equivalent value of SBNs be credited to a third party account?
output: Yes, with specific authorization from the third party and following standard banking procedures.

input: What is the purpose of the Basel III Regulation on Countercyclical Capital Buffer according to the Second Quarter Review of Monetary Policy 2013-14?
output: The review states that the purpose is to operationalise the countercyclical capital buffer framework in India.

input: What is the penalty for shortages in soiled note remittances and currency chest balances for notes in denomination up to ₹50?
output: The penalty for shortages in soiled note remittances and currency chest balances for notes in denomination up to ₹50 is ₹50 per piece in addition to the loss.

input: Can Indian exporters receive advance payment against exports in INR?
output: Yes, Indian exporters may receive advance payment against exports from overseas importers in Indian rupees through the Rupee Payment Mechanism, subject to certain conditions.

input: What is the significance of adhering to the calendar of reviews outlined in the RBI circular?
output: Adhering to the calendar of reviews ensures that private sector banks are meeting the critical minimum requirements of review set by the RBI. It also allows for additional reviews to be prescribed by the bank's Board as needed.

input: When was the circular on Priority Sector Lending for UCBs issued and what does it entail?
output: The circular was issued on July 11, 2006 and it entails changes in the eligibility of investments made by UCBs in bonds issued by NHB/HUDCO for inclusion under Priority Sector advances.

input: Can banks include all types of investments in the computation of IFR?
output: No, banks should only include investments in the "Held for Trading" and "Available for Sale" categories in the computation of IFR. Investments under the "Held to Maturity" category, which are not meant to be traded, should not be included.

input: How should banks prepare for the revised provisioning norm that becomes effective from March 31, 2005?
