output: The amount of provisions required to be held shall be equal to the contract-wise aggregate of 'amount of invocation' after adjusting the realisable value of the assets held by the company in respect of each housing loan where the guarantee has been invoked.

input: What is the purpose of the modified para 3 of the RBI circular dated May 30, 2002?
output: The purpose of the modified para 3 of the RBI circular is to redefine the term "wilful default" and to provide a clear definition of the events that would be considered as wilful default.

input: What is the proposed change in the eligibility norms for opening on-site ATMs for UCBs according to the Annual Policy Statement for the year 2008-09?
output: The proposed change is to dispense with the extant eligibility norms for opening on-site ATMs for well-managed and financially sound UCBs in states that have signed MoUs with RBI or are registered under the Multi-state Cooperative Societies Act, 2002.

input: What method will be used for the auction of Government of India Dated Securities?
output: The auction will be price based using the multiple price method.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular DBOD.No.Leg.BC. 98/09.11.013/2004-05 dated June 24, 2005?
output: The purpose of the circular is to exempt the line of credit / overdraft facility extended by settlement bankers to National Securities Clearing Corporation Ltd. (NSCCL) from the purview of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

input: What is the role of AD Category-I banks in regards to this circular?
output: AD Category-I banks are responsible for bringing the contents of this circular to the notice of their constituents and customers.

input: How has the Reserve Bank of India been involved in monitoring customer service in State/District Central Co-operative Banks (StCBs/DCCBs)?
output: The Reserve Bank of India, as the regulator of StCBs/DCCBs, has been actively engaged in reviewing, examining, and evaluating customer service in these banks. It has issued various guidelines from time to time to ensure the quality of customer service in line with the increasing expectations of customers.

input: What was the purpose of the UAT of IDPMS?
output: The purpose of the UAT was to familiarize AD banks with the system and to facilitate uploading of data related to all outstanding import remittances.

input: What is the target for lending to micro enterprises by RRBs?
output: RRBs have a target of 7.5 per cent of their total outstanding advances for lending to micro enterprises.

input: Will the RBI review these guidelines periodically?
output: Yes, the RBI will review these guidelines periodically to consider modifications and continuance, as appropriate.

input: What happens if the maturity period for NRE Term deposits exceeds three years?
output: The interest rate for NRE Term deposits will remain the same, even if the maturity period exceeds three years.

input: What is the deadline for depositing the requisite amount for the non-competitive bid in the auction of "7.38 per cent Government Stock 2015"?
output: The bidder must deposit the requisite amount at Reserve Bank of India, Fort, Mumbai on the day/time as indicated in the letter of acceptance received from the RBI.

input: What are some of the charges associated with debit cards that banks are required to display?
output: Some of the charges associated with debit cards that banks are required to display include membership fees, annual fees, late payment charges, and cash withdrawal fees.

input: What is the role of State Level Bankers' Committees (SLBCs) in the provision of banking services in villages?
output: SLBCs are mandated to prepare a roadmap for the provision of banking services in all unbanked villages with a population below 2000 and monitor the progress in achieving the targets.

input: What action should be taken by banks and financial institutions in case an existing account is found to be held by or linked to any of the entities or individuals included in the consolidated list?
output: Banks and financial institutions should scan all existing accounts to ensure that no account is held by or linked to any of the entities or individuals included in the consolidated list. If any such account is found, appropriate action should be taken as per the procedure laid down in the UAPA Order dated August 27, 2009.

input: What is the maximum amount of remittance allowed per calendar year for a person specified in sub-regulation (2) and (3) of Regulation 4?
output: The maximum amount of remittance allowed per calendar year is US $ 1,00,000 (US Dollar One lakh only).

input: What are the guidelines issued by RBI for framing the Fair Practices Code for Lenders?
output: RBI has issued guidelines to banks and financial institutions for framing the Fair Practices Code for Lenders, which includes information about fees/charges, pre-payment options, and other matters that affect the interest of the borrower.

input: What is the eligibility criteria for participating in the auction on a non-competitive basis?
output: Investors who do not maintain current account (CA) or Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) account with the Reserve Bank of India and make a single bid for an amount not more than Rs. one crore (face value) per auction are eligible to participate on a non-competitive basis.

input: What is the responsibility of banks in case of customer complaints related to transactions made without additional authentication after the stipulated date?
output: As per the RBI notification, banks are required to reimburse any losses incurred by customers due to transactions made without additional authentication after the stipulated date.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular on Secondary market transactions in Government Securities – Notional Short Sale?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to provide guidelines for market participants undertaking 'notional' short sale in the secondary market for government securities.

input: Can an investor submit more than one competitive bid?
output: Yes, an investor can submit more than one competitive bid at different prices, but the aggregate amount of bids should not exceed the notified amount of the auction.

input: What are the guidelines for valuation of properties and appointment of valuers that banks should follow?
output: Banks should have a Board approved policy for valuation of properties and appoint professionally qualified independent valuers. They should also obtain at least two independent valuation reports for properties valued at Rs.50 crore or above.

input: What is the significance of the rupee value of the special currency basket in the context of the Deferred Payment Protocols between India and USSR?
output: The rupee value of the special currency basket is an important factor in determining the exchange rate between India and USSR under the Deferred Payment Protocols.

input: What is the recommended timeline for publishing Y2K readiness information?
output: The recommended timeline for publishing Y2K readiness information is in the months of June, September, and December 1999. Institutions that have not yet published their Annual Reports for the Year ended March 1999 are also encouraged to make voluntary disclosures in their Director's Report.

input: Who is responsible for ensuring adherence to the guidelines mentioned in the circular by agents and franchisees of authorized persons?
output: It is the sole responsibility of the franchisers to ensure that their agents and franchisees also adhere to the guidelines mentioned in the circular.

input: What is the interest rate for the Government Stock and when will it be paid?
output: The interest rate for the Government Stock is 10.25% per annum and it will be paid half yearly on November 30 and May 30.

input: Will the Government Stocks be eligible for "When Issued" trading?
output: Yes, the Government Stocks will be eligible for "When Issued" trading for a period commencing from March 31-April 2, 2009.

input: What is the interest rate charged by banks on rupee pre-shipment credit and post-shipment credit for the period April 1, 2007 to December 31, 2007?
output: For the period April 1, 2007 to December 31, 2007, banks will charge interest rate not exceeding BPLR minus 4.5% on pre-shipment credit upto 180 days and post-shipment credit upto 90 days on the outstanding amount.

input: What is the deadline for reporting March Residual transactions to Zonal Accounts Officers/Pay and Accounts Officers?
output: The deadline for reporting March Residual transactions to Zonal Accounts Officers/Pay and Accounts Officers is April 18, 2022.

input: What should a tenderer do if their bid is accepted?
output: If a tenderer's bid is accepted, they should collect the letter of acceptance from the Reserve Bank of India, Fort, Mumbai Office.

input: How will the non-competitive bids be allotted?
output: Non-competitive bids will be allotted at the cut-off price that will emerge in the auction on the basis of competitive bidding.

input: What is the limit on the number of subscribers for NCDs with a maximum subscription of less than Rs. 1 crore?
output: The limit on the number of subscribers for NCDs with a maximum subscription of less than Rs. 1 crore is 200 per financial year.

input: What specific considerations should banks take into account while engaging recovery agents?
output: Banks are advised to have a due diligence process in place for engagement of recovery agents, inform the borrower about the details of the recovery agency, and ensure that the agents are properly trained and adhere to guidelines and codes of conduct.

input: Under which sections of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 has this circular been issued?
output: This circular has been issued under Sections 10(4) and 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

input: When will the interest on the Government Stock be paid?
output: The interest at the rate of 6.57 percent per annum will be paid half-yearly on August 14 and February 14.

input: In what cases does a person need to obtain prior permission from the Reserve Bank for remittance of assets?
output: A person needs to obtain prior permission from the Reserve Bank for remittance on account of legacy, bequest or inheritance to a citizen of foreign state permanently resident outside India, remittance to a person resident outside India on the ground of hardship, and remittance of winding up proceeds of a branch/office in India of a person resident outside India.

input: How will the Government Stocks be issued?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued by credit to Subsidiary General Ledger Account (SGL) of parties maintaining such account with Reserve Bank of India or in the form of Stock Certificate.

input: What action is RBI advising banks to take regarding the implementation of RBIA?
output: RBI is advising banks to review their methodology of conducting risk-based internal audit and align it with the guidelines issued by RBI. They should also form a Task Force to expedite the process and ensure compliance with the guidelines.

input: What is the asset classification and provisioning requirement for an account where there is a change of promoter?
output: In case of a change of promoter, the asset classification and provisioning requirement will be as per the Strategic Debt Restructuring (SDR) scheme or the Prudential Norms on Change in Ownership of Borrowing Entities (Outside SDR Scheme).

input: What is the maximum interest rate that NBFCs can pay on public deposits?
output: The maximum interest rate that NBFCs can pay on public deposits is 12.5% per annum.

input: Who is allowed to lend to FPIs for the purpose of placing margins with CCIL?
output: Banks in India having an Authorised Dealer Category-1 licence under FEMA, 1999 are allowed to lend to FPIs for the purpose of placing margins with CCIL.

input: What factors should be considered by NBFCs while determining the rate of interest to be charged for loans and advances?
output: NBFCs should consider factors such as cost of funds, margin, and risk premium while determining the rate of interest to be charged for loans and advances.

input: What is the effective date of the Credit Agreement under the Line of Credit?
output: The effective date of the Credit Agreement under the Line of Credit is September 19, 2008.

input: What is the full form of PK?
output: PK stands for P. Krishnamurthy, the Chief General Manager-in-Charge who signed the notification.

input: What are the key decisions taken by the MPC at its meeting on April 8, 2022?
output: The MPC decided to keep the policy repo rate under the liquidity adjustment facility (LAF) unchanged at 4.0 per cent. The marginal standing facility (MSF) rate and the Bank Rate also remain unchanged at 4.25 per cent. The standing deposit facility (SDF) rate, which will now be the floor of the LAF corridor, will be at 3.75 per cent. The MPC also decided to remain accommodative while focusing on withdrawal of accommodation to ensure that inflation remains within the target going forward, while supporting growth.

input: What is the projected GDP growth for 2018-19, as announced by the MPC in the Fifth Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement, 2018-19?
output: The projected GDP growth for 2018-19, as announced by the MPC in the Fifth Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement, 2018-19, is 7.4 per cent.

input: How will the auction for the sale of Government Stock 2023 be conducted?
output: The auction will be conducted by Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 on August 12, 2016 through a price based auction using multiple price auction method.

input: What is the format for disclosing the movement of provisions in the 'Notes on Accounts' section of banks' balance sheets?
output: The format includes details of opening and closing balances, provisions made during the year, write-offs and transfers to investment fluctuation reserve account, as per the Annexure provided in the RBI notification.

input: Which goods are now allowed to be exported on consignment basis to the Russian Federation according to the RBI circular?
output: Soaps, detergents, toiletries, Lead Acid Cells and Batteries are now allowed to be exported on consignment basis to the Russian Federation according to the RBI circular.

input: When will the increased CRR for Non-Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks come into effect?
output: The increased CRR for Non-Scheduled Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks will come into effect from the fortnight beginning July 12, 2014.

input: What is the terminology used in the Master Circular?
output: The terminology used in the Master Circular includes 'NBFCs' (Non-Banking Financial Companies), 'RNBCs' (Residuary Non-Banking Companies), 'current investments', 'long term investments', and 'unsecured loans'.

input: What are the Accounting Standards (AS) that banks are required to comply with, as per the revised listing agreement with stock exchanges?
output: Banks are required to comply with all the Accounting Standards (AS) issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) from time to time, as per the revised listing agreement with stock exchanges.

input: What is the reporting requirement for FII and QFI investments made prior to registration as RFPI?
output: All investments made by FII and QFI in accordance with the regulations prior to registration as RFPI shall continue to be valid and taken into account for computation of aggregate limit.

input: Can an individual Non-Resident Indian (NRI) invest in the Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme 2019-20?
output: Yes, an individual NRI can invest in the Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme 2019-20. However, the investment must be held until early redemption or maturity and the interest and maturity proceeds are not repatriable.

input: What is the significance of the Foreign Exchange Management (International Financial Services Centre) Regulations, 2015?
output: The Foreign Exchange Management (International Financial Services Centre) Regulations, 2015 provide the legal framework for conducting financial activities in International Financial Services Centres (IFSCs).

input: What is the new frequency of reporting data on defaulters and wilful defaulters to credit information companies?
output: Banks and financial institutions are now required to report data on defaulters and wilful defaulters to credit information companies on a monthly or more frequent basis.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction for the Government Stock?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction for the Government Stock, with a maximum allotment of 5% of the notified amount.

input: Can the nominated agency or bank avail of gold loans from any other entity besides the overseas supplier?
output: No, the loan must be obtained directly from the overseas supplier.

input: What was the announcement made in the Monetary and Credit Policy for the year 2003-2004 regarding transfer of bank branches?
output: The announcement stated that bank branches in rural and semi-urban centres can be transferred from one commercial bank to another.

input: Who should acknowledge receipt of the RBI circular and to whom?
output: The Compliance Officer/Principal Officer of the banks should acknowledge receipt of the circular to the concerned Regional Office of the RBI.

input: What are the revised guidelines for foreign investments in preference shares?
output: The revised guidelines for foreign investments in preference shares were notified by the Government of India on April 30, 2007.

input: Are there any other permissions or approvals required for this Line of Credit?
output: The circular states that the directions are without prejudice to any other permissions or approvals required under any other law.

input: What are the documents required to be executed for availing export credit refinance facility?
output: The banks have to execute a stamped agreement, a Demand Promissory Note (DPN), and a Board Resolution authorizing the borrowing under the scheme. For enhancement of limit, a letter extending the agreement to enhance limit is also required.

input: What happens if the contravener fails to pay the sum for which the contravention was compounded within the specified time?
output: If the contravener fails to pay the sum within the specified time, the compounding application will be deemed to have never been made.

input: When will the modifications to the ECB guidelines for NBFC-AFCs come into effect?
output: The modifications will come into effect immediately.

input: Can Regional Rural Banks and Cooperative Banks submit non-competitive bids directly?
output: Yes, Regional Rural Banks and Cooperative Banks that maintain SGL account and current account with the Reserve Bank of India can submit non-competitive bids directly.

input: What is the purpose of the Banking Companies (Nomination) Rules, 1985?
output: The purpose of the Banking Companies (Nomination) Rules, 1985 is to provide a framework for the nomination of individuals to receive the benefits of a bank account in case of the account holder's death.

input: When did the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme become effective?
output: The Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme became effective from the fortnight beginning on May 07, 2011.

input: What is the maximum amount that an equipment leasing company or hire purchase finance company accepting public deposit can invest in land or building?
output: An equipment leasing company or hire purchase finance company accepting public deposit can invest up to 10% of its owned fund in land or building, except for its own use.

input: Are banks allowed to renew swap transactions for Tier I/Tier II bonds?
output: No, banks are advised not to renew swap transactions for Tier I/Tier II bonds upon their expiry.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction and what is the allotted amount for non-competitive bidders?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction. The Government Stock up to 5% of the notified amount of the sale will be allotted to non-competitive bidders as per the Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in the Auctions of Government Securities.

input: What happens in case the aggregate amount of bids is more than the reserved amount?
output: In case the aggregate amount of bids is more than the reserved amount, pro rata allotment would be made.

input: What are the conditions for making advance remittance for import of rough diamonds?
output: The conditions include submission of a report in the prescribed format, as mentioned in the A. P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 34 dated March 02, 2007. These conditions are applicable to all the permitted mining companies.

input: What is the process for printing the Holding Certificate for the Sovereign Gold Bonds?
output: The Holding Certificate needs to be printed in color on A4 size 100 GSM paper.

input: Is there a specific form or declaration required for premature encashment of the 6.5% Savings Bonds, 2003?
output: No, there is no specific form or declaration required for premature encashment of the 6.5% Savings Bonds, 2003.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular regarding the list of Terrorist Individuals/Organisations under UNSCR 1267(1999) and 1822(2008) on Taliban/Al-Qaida Organisation?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to inform all Scheduled Commercial Banks, Local Area Banks, and All India Financial Institutions about the changes made in the Consolidated List of Individuals and entities linked to Al-Qaida and Taliban, and to ensure compliance with the UAPA Order issued by the Government.

input: Can NRIs make payments to utility service providers in India through Exchange Houses?
output: Yes, NRIs can make payments to utility service providers in India through Exchange Houses for services such as water supply, electricity supply, telephone, internet, and television.

input: How much primary liquidity will be injected into the banking system due to the CRR reduction?
output: The CRR reduction will inject around `480 billion of primary liquidity into the banking system.

input: When will the auctions be conducted?
output: The auctions will be conducted on May 14, 2010.

input: What is the purpose of Part - I of the Diligence Report?
output: Part - I of the Diligence Report is for the Chartered Accountant to provide a report on the management and shareholding pattern of the company, any changes that took place during the period, and alterations to the Memorandum and Articles of Association.

input: What is the interest rate applicable for Export Credit Not Otherwise Specified (ECNOS)?
output: For Pre-shipment credit, the interest rate for ECNOS is free, and for post-shipment credit, the interest rate is also free.

input: What steps should banks take to ensure electronic filing of cash transaction reports and suspicious transaction reports to FIU-IND?
output: Banks should initiate urgent steps to ensure electronic filing of cash transaction reports and suspicious transaction reports to FIU-IND.

input: How often will the interest be paid on the Government Stock and when will it be repaid?
output: Interest will be paid half yearly on the nominal value of the Government Stock and it will be repaid at par on the date of maturity.

input: What is the procedure for reporting March transactions beginning from April 1, 2007?
output: The Nodal/Focal Point branches should segregate scrolls/challans received from receiving branches on a daily basis and prepare separate main scrolls for March 2007 transactions and current transactions.

input: What information and documents need to be furnished to the Reserve Bank of India for sanction of the amalgamation between two banking companies?
output: The information and documents specified in Annexure A, including the draft scheme of amalgamation, notices of shareholder meetings, and certificates from officers certifying the resolution and number of votes.

input: What are the conditions for interoperability at the retail outlets or sub-agents of BCs?
output: The conditions for interoperability at the retail outlets or sub-agents of BCs include carrying out transactions and authentications online, using the Core Banking Solution (CBS) platform, and following standard operating procedures advised by the Indian Banks' Association (IBA).

input: Can CICs undertake insurance agency business?
output: No, CICs cannot undertake insurance agency business.

input: Can NGOs engaged in micro finance activities avail of ECBs?
output: Yes, NGOs engaged in micro finance activities can avail of ECBs up to USD 10 million per financial year under the automatic route.

input: Can the Government Stock be converted into Stock of any other maturity?
output: Yes, the Government Stock will be eligible for conversion into Stock of any other maturity subject to the conditions laid down by the Government of India.

input: What is the method of auction being used for the sale of Government of India Dated Securities?
output: The Government of India is using a price-based auction using uniform price method for the sale of 7.99 percent Government Stock 2017, Floating Rate Bonds 2020, and 9.15 percent Government Stock 2024. For New 30 Year Government Stock 2041, a yield-based auction using uniform price method will be used.

input: What is the maximum limit for banks' investments in subsidiaries and non-subsidiary financial services companies?
output: The maximum limit for banks' investments in subsidiaries and non-subsidiary financial services companies is 20% of the bank's paid-up share capital and reserves.

input: What is the current all-in-cost ceiling for Trade Credits as specified in the circular?
output: The current all-in-cost ceiling for Trade Credits is applicable till June 30, 2013, and will be subject to review thereafter.

input: Can scheduled UCBs open mobile/satellite offices?
output: Yes, scheduled UCBs can open mobile/satellite offices if they comply with the eligibility criteria.

input: What is the RBI's mission at this juncture?
output: The RBI's mission at this juncture is to manage a durable, strong, and inclusive recovery, while fortifying macroeconomic fundamentals and making financial markets and institutions resilient and sound.

input: When will the auction take place and when will the payment for the stock be made?
output: The auction will take place on July 18, 2007, and the payment for the stock will be made on July 19, 2007.

input: iii) Incentives will be paid only on the machines installed in the branches / currency chests which are in operation and are functional.iv) The reimbursement will be made by the respective Issue Office of RBI within 60 days from the date of receipt of the claim from the bank.v) The banks should ensure that the machines are used for the purpose for which they are installed and not for any other purpose. In case of any misuse of the machines, the banks shall be liable to refund the entire amount of reimbursement to the RBI.vi) The machines should be maintained in good working condition and should be replenished with adequate cash. The machines should be periodically audited by the banks to ensure that they are functioning properly.3.3 Monitoring of the Schemei) The performance of the banks under the scheme will be monitored by the RBI through the inspection of currency chests and incognito visits to bank branches.ii) The performance of the banks will also be reviewed by the RBI at half yearly intervals.iii) The RBI may also review the scheme from time to time and make necessary changes, if required.4. General Instructionsi) The banks should submit their Annual Plans for the next financial year to the respective Issue Office of RBI latest by April 15 of every year.ii) The banks should submit their claims for reimbursement of incentives on quarterly basis within 30 days from the close of the respective quarter.iii) The banks should ensure that the machines are used for the purpose for which they are installed and not for any other purpose. In case of any misuse of the machines, the banks shall be liable to refund the entire amount of reimbursement to the RBI.iv) The banks should maintain proper records of the machines installed and the claims submitted for reimbursement.v) The banks should comply with the instructions contained in the Master Direction on Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme (CDES) and any other instructions issued by the RBI from time to time.1. What is the purpose of the Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme (CDES)?
output: The CDES has been formulated to ensure that all bank branches provide better customer service to members of the public with regard to exchange of notes and coins, in keeping with the objectives of Clean Note Policy.

input: Which banks are excluded from the RBI circular on Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification?
output: Regional Rural Banks and Small Finance Banks are excluded from the RBI circular on Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification.

input: How will deposits placed with NABARD / SIDBI be treated in terms of risk weight?
output: All deposits placed with NABARD / SIDBI in lieu of shortfall in advances to priority sector will attract 100% risk weight since these deposits are in lieu of shortfall in assets which carry 100% risk weight.

input: Why has the RBI decided to extend the On Tap Special Long-Term Repo Operations (SLTRO) facility till December 31, 2021?
output: The RBI has decided to extend the On Tap Special Long-Term Repo Operations (SLTRO) facility till December 31, 2021, to provide continued support to small business units, micro and small industries, and other unorganised sector entities, which have been adversely affected by the pandemic.

input: How long will the exemptions be valid for?
output: The exemptions will be valid for deposits raised till November 04, 2022, and the exemption on reserves maintenance will be available for the original deposit amounts until the deposits are held in the bank books.

input: What are the risks associated with upfront disbursal of housing loans in the housing sector?
output: The risks associated with upfront disbursal of housing loans include exposure to disputes between individual borrowers and developers/builders, default or delayed payment of interest/EMI by the developer/builder, non-completion of the project on time, and potential diversion of funds.

input: Are there any exceptions to the new realization period for export proceeds?
output: Yes, the provisions for the period of realization and repatriation of export proceeds for units situated in Special Economic Zones (SEZ) and exports made to warehouses established outside India remain unchanged.

input: What changes have been made to the Master Circular on Branch Licensing for RRBs?
output: The RBI has modified the Master Circular on Branch Licensing for RRBs to dispense with the Service Area Obligations.

input: How much has the CRR been increased for Scheduled UCBs and in how many stages?
output: The CRR has been increased by 50 basis points to 8.75% for Scheduled UCBs in two stages. The first stage will be effective from July 05, 2008 with a CRR of 8.50% and the second stage will be effective from July 19, 2008 with a CRR of 8.75%.

input: What is the deadline for FIs to phase out excess exposures beyond the prescribed limits?
output: FIs must phase out excess exposures beyond the prescribed limits by March 31, 2005.

input: What are the conditions for UCBs to accept e-Aadhaar as an officially valid document?
output: The conditions for UCBs to accept e-Aadhaar as an officially valid document are:

input: Are authorized persons prohibited from conducting transactions with countries and jurisdictions mentioned in the circular?
output: No, authorized persons are not prohibited from conducting legitimate transactions with these countries and jurisdictions. However, they are advised to consider the information contained in the circular.

input: Can NRIs remit their current income without maintaining an NRO account in India?
output: Yes, NRIs can now remit their current income without maintaining an NRO account in India, based on an appropriate certification by a Chartered Accountant.

input: When do the changes in the Return on Capital Adequacy become applicable?
output: The changes become applicable from the quarter ended March 31, 2005.

input: What are the details to be furnished along with the application for compounding of contravention relating to Foreign Direct Investment in India?
output: The details to be furnished include the name of the applicant, date of incorporation, nature of activities undertaken, details of foreign investor, and details of foreign inward remittances received.

input: When will the auction for the Government Stock take place and what are the timings for submission of bids?
output: The auction will take place on November 20, 2015, and bids should be submitted in electronic format on the Reserve Bank of India Core Banking Solution (E-Kuber) system between 10.30 a.m. and 12.00 noon. Non-competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m. on the same day.

input: What is the directive issued by RBI under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007?
output: The directive issued by RBI under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007 is for banks to put in place their own Board approved policy on merchant acquisition in order to expand card acceptance infrastructure.

input: What is the maximum percentage of the notified amount that will be accepted for non-competitive bids in the auctions of dated securities?
output: Non-competitive bids up to 5 percent of the notified amount will be accepted in the auctions of dated securities.

input: What is the time limit for an Indian entity to submit evidence of investment in a foreign entity?
output: An Indian entity must submit evidence of investment within six months from the date of effecting remittance or the date on which the dues to such person are capitalised or the date on which the amount due was allowed to be capitalised, whichever is earlier.

input: What is the purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign exchange derivative contracts) (Amendment) Regulations, 2003?
output: The purpose of the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign exchange derivative contracts) (Amendment) Regulations, 2003 is to amend the existing regulations related to foreign exchange derivative contracts, in accordance with the powers conferred by the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.

input: What is the purpose of Part D in SCRC 1?
output: Part D provides general information based on the acquisition value of assets.

input: What is the purpose of the directive issued by the RBI?
output: The purpose of the directive issued by the RBI is to modify its previous directive and widen the interest subvention scheme for rupee export credit to include additional sectors and tariff lines.

input: What is the change in reporting requirements for Authorized Dealer Banks according to RBI Circular No. 50?
output: RBI Circular No. 50 states that Authorized Dealer Banks are no longer required to submit Form ORA to the Regional Offices of Reserve Bank on a half-yearly basis. Instead, they are instructed to maintain the particulars of approvals granted for opening of Trading Office / Non-Trading Office / Branch Office/ Representative Office at their end.

input: Who was consulted in the finalization of these guidelines?
output: The guidelines were finalized in consultation with the Foreign Exchange Dealers Association of India (FEDAI) and market participants.

input: What will be taken into account for the purpose of consolidation if the Board of Directors does not record the intent of holding the investment at the time of acquisition?
output: In the absence of record of such intent by the Board at the time of investment, the same will be taken into account for the purpose of consolidation.

input: What is the date of effect for the amended definition of 'Infrastructure Lending'?
output: The date of effect for the amended definition of 'Infrastructure Lending' is the date of the circular, i.e. November 29, 2013.

input: What is the purpose of the circular IECD/5/08.12.01/2000-01?
output: The circular, issued on October 16, 2000, and reiterated on May 30, 2003, advises banks to fix separate sub-limits within the overall limits for large corporate borrowers to meet payment obligations to SSIs.

input: What is the full form of Pay and Accounts Officers?
output: Pay and Accounts Officers stands for Pay and Accounts Officers.

input: How much has the CRR been reduced by and what is the new percentage?
output: The CRR has been reduced by 150 basis points, from 9.00% to 7.50% of net demand and time liabilities (NDTL).

input: What is the time period within which a person must sell the realised foreign exchange to an authorised person?
output: The person must sell the realised foreign exchange within seven days from the date of its receipt if it is remuneration for services rendered, or within ninety days in all other cases.

input: Can a non-resident Indian close relative be a joint holder with more than one resident in the same account?
output: Yes, a non-resident Indian close relative can be a joint holder with more than one resident in the same account, as long as they are a close relative of all the resident account holders.

input: How can banks and financial institutions access the complete details of the updated Al Qaida Sanctions List?
output: The complete details of the updated Al Qaida Sanctions List are available on the UN website at http://www.un.org/sc/committees/1267/aq_sanctions_list.shtml.

input: What is the time period for submitting non-competitive bids?
output: Non-competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m. on the day of the auction.

input: Are all other instructions specified in the previous circular applicable to this circular as well?
output: Yes, all other instructions specified in the previous circular dated January 1, 2019 are applicable to this circular as well.

input: Why has RBI decided to extend the banking hours for conducting government business on March 30 and 31, 2012?
output: RBI has decided to extend the banking hours on March 30 and 31, 2012 to facilitate receipt of government revenue from members of the public even at late hours and to ensure all government transactions are accounted for by the end of the financial year.

input: What action should be taken by banks regarding freezing of funds, financial assets, or economic resources of designated individuals/entities according to the RBI circular?
output: Banks should take action as detailed in paragraph 6 of the circular dated September 17, 2009, which includes freezing of funds, financial assets, or economic resources held in the form of bank accounts of the designated individuals/entities.

input: What is the purpose of the Collateralised Lending Facility (CLF) and Additional Collateralised Lending Facility (ACLF) provided by the RBI to scheduled commercial banks?
output: The purpose of CLF and ACLF is to meet the liquidity requirements of banks and the system.

input: What is the effective date of the revised LIM?
output: The revised LIM came into force with immediate effect on the date of the circular, i.e. January 17, 2003.

input: What is the deadline for submitting a report on the buyback process to the RBI?
output: A report on the buyback process must be submitted to the RBI after completion of the buyback, through the designated AD Category - I bank.

input: Can banks grant advances against fixed deposit receipts or other term deposits of other banks?
output: No, banks are not allowed to grant advances against fixed deposit receipts or other term deposits of other banks.

input: Are there any exposure norms for the NOFHC and the bank?
output: Yes, the NOFHC and the bank shall not have any exposure to the Promoter Group.

input: What is the method of issue for the sale of the Government Stock?
output: The Government Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner prescribed in the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008 by a price based auction using multiple price auction method.

input: Who is responsible for ensuring compliance with the instructions contained in A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No.49?
output: Authorised dealers in foreign exchange are responsible for ensuring compliance with the instructions contained in A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No.49.

input: What is the eligibility criteria for export of goods and services under the LoC?
output: The goods and services must be eligible for export under the Foreign Trade Policy of the Government of India and must be agreed to be financed by the Exim Bank under the agreement.

input: What is the base rate for the first coupon period of the Floating Rate Bonds, 2014?
output: The base rate for the first coupon period is 4.95% (as per the enclosed illustration).

input: Will there be any changes in reporting for other inter-bank OTC foreign exchange derivatives and trades with clients?
output: Yes, reporting for other inter-bank OTC foreign exchange derivatives and all/selective trades with clients will be introduced in a phase-wise manner, as advised by the RBI in due course.

input: What is the new directive given by RBI regarding the shifting of branches?
output: RBI has advised banks to proceed with the shifting of branches within municipal wards/localities in metropolitan/urban and semi-urban centers, and within the same block/service area in rural areas without seeking prior approval. However, banks must ensure that the new address is incorporated in the license within three months of the shifting.

input: How should banks and financial institutions update their list of individuals and entities in light of the changes made in the UNSCR 1988(2011) Sanctions List?
output: Banks and financial institutions are required to update their list of individuals and entities as circulated by the RBI and ensure that no new account is opened for any individual or entity included in the list. They should also scan all existing accounts to ensure that none are held by or linked to any designated individual or entity.

input: What is the review timeline for the new policy on integrated township development?
output: The policy will be reviewed in June 2009.

input: What is the new segment introduced for reporting SHG lending by banks?
output: The new segment is 'Advances to SHGs' and it is a part of the priority sector.

input: What is the procedure that banks are required to follow in regards to the updated consolidated list of individuals/entities?
output: Banks are required to update the consolidated list of individuals/entities as circulated by the RBI and ensure that no account is held by or linked to any of the entities or individuals included in the list. They are also advised to strictly follow the procedure laid down in the UAPA Order dated August 27, 2009 and ensure meticulous compliance to the Order issued by the Government.

input: What is the method of valuation specified by the RBI for SLR securities?
output: The method of valuation specified by the RBI for SLR securities is the current market price.

input: According to Section 15(1) of the Act, what is the requirement for credit institutions?
output: According to Section 15(1) of the Act, every credit institution must become a member of at least one credit information company within three months from the commencement of the Act.

input: What is the responsibility of FPIs and custodians in complying with the limits for investment in G-secs and SDLs according to the circular?
output: The primary responsibility of complying with all limits for investment in G-secs and SDLs lies with the FPIs and custodians.

input: When and where will the auction take place and what is the tenure of the Government Stock?
output: The auction will take place on July 27, 2006 at Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 and the tenure of the Government Stock will be eight years, starting from May 14, 2002.

input: What is the significance of the UN Security Council Resolutions No. 1173, 1176 and 1343(2001) mentioned in the undertaking?
output: These resolutions ban the trade of conflict diamonds and prohibit the import of rough diamonds from certain countries.

input: What is the purpose of the Draft Variation Margin (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2020 released by RBI?
output: The purpose of the Draft Variation Margin (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2020 is to improve the safety of settlement of over-the-counter derivatives that are not centrally cleared, in line with the G-20 recommendations.

input: What will happen if a customer experiences a loss due to a CNP transaction without additional authentication after the stipulated date?
output: In case of customer complaints regarding losses from CNP transactions without additional authentication after the stipulated date, the issuer bank is required to reimburse the customer without any hesitation.

input: What is the standard method for arriving at the value of gold accepted as collateral?
output: The standard method for arriving at the value of gold accepted as collateral is by taking into account the average of the closing price of 22 carat gold for the preceding 30 days as quoted by The Bombay Bullion Association Ltd. (BBA).

input: Are the scrolls for reimbursement of pension to All India Service Officers handled under the Single Window System?
output: No, the scrolls for reimbursement of pension to All India Service Officers are not handled under the Single Window System and should not be sent to the Central Pension Accounting Office (CPAO). Instead, they should be sent to the concerned Accountant General and the Reserve Bank/State Bank for reimbursement.

input: What is the minimum price up to which tenders for purchase of Government Stock will be accepted at the auctions?
output: The minimum price will be determined by the Reserve Bank of India based on the bids received.

input: Are the Government of India Dated Securities eligible for Repurchase transactions (Repo)?
output: Yes, the Government of India Dated Securities are eligible for Repurchase transactions (Repo) in accordance with the Repurchase transactions (Repo) Directions, 2018.

input: How can banks encourage their branch level functionaries to avail of the CGS cover?
output: Banks can encourage their branch level functionaries to avail of the CGS cover by making it a criterion in the evaluation of their performance and by creating widespread awareness about the key features and benefits of the Scheme.

input: What is the risk weight for the amount outstanding in the account named "Amount receivable from Government of India under Agricultural Debt Waiver Scheme 2008"?
output: The amount outstanding in this account is treated as a claim on the Government of India and attracts zero risk weight for the purpose of capital adequacy norms.

input: When will the instructions contained in the RBI's circular on ECB Policy be applicable till?
output: The instructions contained in the circular will be applicable till September 30, 2013 and will be subject to review thereafter.

input: What is the date and time for submission of non-competitive bids for the auction of the 7.59 per cent Government Stock 2029?
output: The non-competitive bids for the auction of the 7.59 per cent Government Stock 2029 should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m. on June 10, 2016.

input: What actions are prohibited for recovery agents employed by regulated entities?
output: Recovery agents employed by regulated entities are prohibited from resorting to intimidation or harassment, making false or misleading representations, and contacting borrowers before 8:00 a.m. or after 7:00 p.m.

input: Can AD Category - I banks engage in proprietary trading in the currency futures / exchange traded currency options markets?
output: No, as per the notification, AD Category - I banks are not allowed to engage in proprietary trading in the currency futures / exchange traded currency options markets.

input: What services should be provided by an e-commerce marketplace entity or other entities in which the marketplace entity has direct or indirect equity participation or common control, to vendors on the platform?
output: Services such as fulfilment, logistics, warehousing, advertisement/marketing, payments, financing, etc. should be provided by an e-commerce marketplace entity or other entities in which the marketplace entity has direct or indirect equity participation or common control, to vendors on the platform at arm's length and in a fair and non-discriminatory manner.

input: How should the ceiling of 15% be reckoned for the purpose of computing the prescribed limit?
output: The ceiling of 15% should be reckoned on the total deposits of a bank based on the audited balance sheet as on March 31 of the previous financial year.

input: What is the purpose of the Ad hoc Committees on Procedures and Performance Audit on Customer Services in Banks?
output: The purpose of the Ad hoc Committees is to improve customer services in relation to various banking services and to safeguard the interests of common persons from any unfair procedural practices by banks.

input: What is the method of issue for the Government Stock being sold through this auction?
output: The Government Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner of a price based auction using multiple price auction method.

input: When will the revised definition of 'infrastructure lending' be effective?
output: The revised definition of 'infrastructure lending' will be effective from the date of the circular.

input: What is the maximum interest rate that scheduled commercial banks can pay on NRI deposits?
output: The maximum interest rate that scheduled commercial banks can pay on NRI deposits is 100 basis points above the LIBOR/SWAP rates for US dollar of the corresponding maturity.

input: What is the significance of the 'Fit and Proper' status of directors?
output: The 'Fit and Proper' status of directors ensures that they possess the necessary qualifications, experience, and character to hold such positions and make sound decisions for the bank.

input: What is the maximum percentage of the notified amount that will be accepted through non-competitive bids in the auctions of dated securities?
output: Non-competitive bids up to 5% of the notified amount will be accepted in the auctions of dated securities.

input: Are there any exceptions to the guidelines on spread charged to an existing borrower?
output: Yes, the guidelines on spread charged to an existing borrower do not apply to loans under consortium/multiple banking arrangements.

input: Is there a difference in interest rates for fixed and floating rate deposits?
output: Yes, there is a difference in interest rates for fixed and floating rate deposits. For fixed rate deposits, the interest rate is determined by adding the LIBOR/SWAP rates plus 125 basis points, while for floating rate deposits, the interest rate is determined by adding the SWAP rates plus 125 basis points.

input: Can banks allow premature withdrawals of fixed/term deposits according to the mandate of 'Either or Survivor' or 'Former or Survivor'?
output: Yes, banks can allow premature withdrawals of fixed/term deposits according to the mandate of 'Either or Survivor' or 'Former or Survivor', provided they have taken a specific joint mandate from the depositors for this purpose.

input: When will the mock auction be conducted and who is requested to participate?
output: The mock auction will be conducted on Sunday, April 3, 2005 and all NDS members are requested to participate.

input: What forms must be used for declaring shipments under the Line of Credit?
output: Shipments under the Line of Credit must be declared on EDF/ SDF Forms as per instructions issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time.

input: What should banks do to facilitate authorities in identifying the ownership of locker keys?
output: Banks should emboss the identification code of the bank/branch on all locker keys.

input: Can exporters avail of pre-shipment credit in foreign currency for all types of exports?
output: No, pre-shipment credit in foreign currency is only applicable for cash exports.

input: Who will issue the operational guidelines for allocation and monitoring of limits as per the circular?
output: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) will issue the operational guidelines for allocation and monitoring of limits as per the circular.

input: What is the revised accounting for investments norm?
output: The earlier bifurcation of long term investments into quoted and unquoted investments has been done away with. Under the modified norms, the unquoted equity shares in the nature of current investments only are required to be valued at cost or break up value.

input: Can cooperative banks use other sources for determining the value of gold jewellery accepted as security/collateral?
output: Yes, cooperative banks can also use the historical spot gold price data of the preceding 30 days publicly disseminated by a Commodity Exchange regulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India.

input: Are there any restrictions on the interest rates offered by banks on NRE and NRO deposits?
output: Yes, the interest rates offered by banks on NRE and NRO deposits cannot be higher than those offered on comparable domestic rupee deposits.

input: What is the process for handling complaints related to ‘significant benchmark’ administration?
output: FBAs must have a formal mechanism for handling complaints related to ‘significant benchmark’ administration and an effective ‘whistleblower’ mechanism to facilitate early detection of potential misconduct or irregularities.

input: What risk management guidelines must Standalone Primary Dealers follow for participation in the currency futures market?
output: Standalone Primary Dealers must lay down detailed guidelines on risk management, including exposure, risk limits, and reporting requirements, with Board's approval for the conduct of this activity and management of risks.

input: When will the result of the auction be announced?
output: The result of the auction will be announced on June 11, 2009.

input: What should banks do in case of doubt regarding the validity of a Legal Guardianship Certificate?
output: In case of doubt, banks should obtain proper legal advice.

input: What is the new sub-rule (1C) added to rule 9 of the Prevention of Money-laundering Rules, 2005?
output: The new sub-rule (1C) prohibits banking companies, financial institutions and Intermediaries from opening or keeping any anonymous or fictitious accounts or accounts on behalf of undisclosed or unverified persons.

input: How often are banks required to reconcile their inter-branch entries according to RBI instructions?
output: Banks are required to reconcile their inter-branch entries within a period of six months, as per RBI instructions.

input: What is the power conferred by sub-section (7) of Section 42 of the RBI Act, 1934?
output: The power conferred by sub-section (7) of Section 42 of the RBI Act, 1934 allows the Reserve Bank of India to exempt scheduled banks from the maintenance of CRR.

input: What is the significance of the circular dated March 3, 2006 mentioned in the RBI circular?
output: The circular dated March 3, 2006 is significant because it contains guidelines and instructions for banks to maintain and preserve information about transactions with their clients, as well as report suspicious transactions to the Director of the Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND).

input: What is the method of issue for the sale of the Government Stock 2024?
output: The Government Stock 2024 will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner prescribed in paragraph 5.1 of the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008 by a price based auction using multiple price auction method.

input: Who will be the convenor of the SSCs?
output: The Lead District Manager (LDM) of the Lead Bank will be designated as the convenor of the SSCs.

input: Who can banks contact for further clarification on these instructions?
output: Banks can contact the Chief General Manager for further clarification on these instructions.

input: What is the requirement for ARCs in regards to customer confidentiality?
output: ARCs are required to keep all information acquired in the course of their business strictly confidential, except when required by law, duty towards the public, or with the borrower's permission.

input: What is the role of Lead Banks in organizing sensitization workshops for bank officials regarding micro credit/lending to SHGs?
output: The Lead Banks may organize sensitization workshops for bank officials regarding micro credit/lending to SHGs with the help of DDMs of NABARD.

input: How often should UCBs review the operations of ATM-cum-debit cards?
output: UCBs should review the operations of these cards and put up review notes to their Boards at half-yearly intervals, at the end of March and September, every year.

input: What is the Voluntary Retention Route (VRR) and when was it introduced?
output: The Voluntary Retention Route (VRR) was introduced on March 01, 2019 with the purpose of facilitating stable investments in debt instruments issued in the country by Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs).

input: What additional preventive measures can banks consider for dealing with suspicious or large value cheques?
output: Banks can consider alerting the customer by phone and contacting the base branch in case of non-home cheques.

input: Who is responsible for overseeing the compliance of internal audit and concurrent audit observations?
output: The Audit Committee of Board is responsible for overseeing the compliance of internal audit and concurrent audit observations.

input: Can auditors be considered for reappointment based on their performance with the bank?
output: The report should indicate whether, in the bank's opinion, the auditors can be considered for reappointment, with reasons if not.

input: What is the procedure for remittances on hardship ground?
output: Remittances on hardship ground require prior approval from the Reserve Bank.

input: What is the significance of the RBI's guidelines for banks in relation to counterparty credit risk?
output: The guidelines provide a standardized approach for computing exposure for counterparty credit risk arising from derivative transactions, which will help banks in managing their credit risk more effectively.

input: How does the practice of bundling charges for merchants hinder regulatory compliance?
output: Bundling charges for merchants makes it difficult to adhere to the extant regulatory mandate and can also lead to indiscriminate passing on of costs to customers in the form of surcharges.

input: How will the increase in provisioning requirement for NPAs in the 'doubtful for more than three years' category be applied?
output: The increase in provisioning requirement for NPAs in the 'doubtful for more than three years' category will be applied in a phased manner over a three year period for the existing stock of NPAs classified as 'doubtful more than three years' as on March 31, 2004.

input: How should the calculation of incremental provisioning and capital requirements be done for projects under implementation and new entities?
output: For projects under implementation, the calculation should be based on projected average EBID for the three years from the date of commencement of commercial operations. For new entities, the same framework may be applied.

input: What is the recommended timeline for other commercial banks (excluding LABs and RRBs) to migrate to the Standardised Approach for credit risk and Basic Indicator Approach for operational risk under the Revised Framework?
output: The recommended timeline for other commercial banks (excluding LABs and RRBs) to migrate to the Standardised Approach for credit risk and Basic Indicator Approach for operational risk under the Revised Framework is March 31, 2009.

input: When and where will the auction be conducted?
output: The auction will be conducted by Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 on December 31, 2013.

input: Can UCBs refer to any guidelines for guidance on cyber security?
output: Yes, UCBs can refer to guidelines issued by CERT-In/NCIIPC/RBI/IDRBT for guidance on cyber security.

input: Are the norms for minimum annual target applicable for new entities applying for PD authorization?
output: Yes, the norms for minimum annual target are also applicable for new entities applying for PD authorization as per IDMD.PCD.9/14.03.05/2011-12 dated August 30, 2011 and para 1.3.2 of RBI's Master circular on Operational Guidelines to PDs dated July 01, 2013.

input: What is the time frame for submitting non-competitive bids and competitive bids?
output: Non-competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 11.30 a.m. and competitive bids should be submitted between 10.30 a.m. and 12.30 p.m.

input: What is the deadline for opening letters of credit and disbursement of credit?
output: The deadline for opening letters of credit is March 20, 2006 and the deadline for disbursement of credit is September 20, 2006.

input: What is the role of Lead District Officers (LDOs) in assessing the impact of financial literacy camps?
output: The Lead District Officers (LDOs) of Reserve Bank of India will assess and evaluate the impact of financial literacy camps on an ongoing basis.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular on KYC norms, AML standards, and CFT?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to inform all AD I category Urban Co-operative Banks about the ongoing review of compliance with AML/CFT standards by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF).

input: What is the purpose of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM) and how does it benefit SCs/STs?
output: The National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM) was launched to provide credit facilities to SCs/STs in urban areas and promote their self-employment.

input: What is the proposed change in provisioning requirements in the future?
output: The proposed change in provisioning requirements in the future is to gradually enhance them.

input: How should shipments under the Line of Credit be declared?
output: Shipments under the Line of Credit must be declared on GR / SDF Forms as per instructions issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time.

input: How often will the interest be paid on the Government Stock?
output: The interest will be payable half-yearly on November 3 and May 3.

input: Who is eligible to participate in the auction for the '8.33 per cent Government Stock, 2026'?
output: Eligible individuals and institutions can participate in the auction for the stock, with a maximum allotment of 5% of the notified amount.

input: How will successful bidders make payment for the Government of India Dated Securities?
output: Payment by successful bidders must be made on June 22, 2009 (Monday).

input: How will the Stock be sold and by whom?
output: The Stock will be sold through Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai- 400 001 in the manner prescribed in the General Notification F. No. 4 (13)–W&M/2008, dated October 8, 2008 by a price based auction using multiple price auction method.

input: What is the responsibility of banks regarding making depositors aware of the applicable penal rates?
output: Banks should ensure that the depositors are made aware of the applicable penal rates along with the deposit rates.

input: How will the auction for the sale of the Government Stock be conducted?
output: The auction will be conducted by the Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 on April 10, 2015. Bids for the auction should be submitted in electronic format on the Reserve Bank of India Core Banking Solution (E-Kuber) system on April 10, 2015.

input: What is the purpose of RBI Circular No. 48 issued on December 09, 2014?
output: The purpose of RBI Circular No. 48 is to inform all Category-I Authorized Dealer Banks about the revised regulations for overseas investments by Indian Alternative Investment Funds (AIF).

input: Can Indian parties set up a holding company or Special Purpose Vehicle abroad under the general permission of Regulation 6?
output: No, Indian parties are not allowed to set up a holding company or Special Purpose Vehicle abroad under the general permission of Regulation 6. They would require prior approval from the Reserve Bank for such investments.

input: What is the notified amount for each type of Government of India Dated Securities being offered in the auction?
output: The notified amount for “8.24 percent Government Stock 2018” is Rs. 3,000 crore, for “8.79 percent Government Stock 2021” is Rs. 6,000 crore, and for “8.83 percent Government Stock 2041” is Rs. 3,000 crore.

input: How will the FC component of export credit be monitored and translated in banks' books under the alternative method suggested by RBI?
output: Under the alternative method, the FC component of export credit will be maintained and monitored in FC, and the ongoing exchange/FEDAI rates will be used for translation of FC assets in the banks' books.

input: What are the conditions that non-competitive bidders must abide by in order to participate in the auction?
output: Non-competitive bidders must abide by the terms and conditions stated in the 'Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in the Auctions of Government Securities' mentioned in the notification.

input: Which districts in Assam will have a change in the Lead Bank responsibilities?
output: All districts in Assam will have a change in the Lead Bank responsibilities.

input: Can banks offer different interest rates for deposits below Rs.15 lakh of the same maturity?
output: No, banks cannot offer different interest rates for deposits below Rs.15 lakh of the same maturity.

input: Who has the authority to make amendments to the Foreign Exchange Management (Export of Goods and Services) Regulations, 2000?
output: The Reserve Bank of India has the authority to make amendments to the Foreign Exchange Management (Export of Goods and Services) Regulations, 2000, in accordance with the powers conferred by the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.

input: When will the payment for the ‘7.83 per cent Government Stock, 2018’ be made and what does it include?
output: The payment for the ‘7.83 per cent Government Stock, 2018’ will be made on May 30, 2011, which is the date of issue. It will include accrued interest on the nominal value of the Stock allotted in the auction from the date of original issue, i.e., April 11, 2011 to May 29, 2011.

input: What is the minimum amount for which the Government Stocks will be issued?
output: The Government Stocks will be issued for a minimum amount of Rs.10,000/- (nominal) and in multiples of Rs.10,000/- thereafter.

input: Can an investor submit more than one competitive bid for the auction?
output: Yes, an investor can submit more than one competitive bid at different prices, but the total amount of bids should not exceed the notified amount of auction.

input: What is the method of auction being used for the Government of India Dated Securities?
output: The auction will be conducted through a price-based auction using the uniform price method.

input: What is the tenure of the '7.16 per cent Government Stock, 2023' and when will it be repaid?
output: The tenure of the Stock is ten years, commencing from May 20, 2013. It will be repaid at par on May 20, 2023.

input: What is the tenure of the 8.13 per cent Government Stock 2045 and when will it be repaid?
output: The Government Stock will have a tenure of thirty years, commencing from June 22, 2015 and will be repaid at par on June 22, 2045.

input: What are the conditions for banks and NBFCs to restructure loans for MSMEs without downgrading their asset classification?
output: The conditions for banks and NBFCs to restructure loans for MSMEs without downgrading their asset classification are: aggregate exposure not exceeding ₹25 crore, borrower's account being a 'standard asset' as of March 1, 2020, implementation of restructuring by March 31, 2021, and the borrower being GST-registered (except for exempt MSMEs).

input: What is the recommended frequency for reporting compliance failures to the Board?
output: Instances of material compliance failures should be reported promptly to the Board on a quarterly basis.

input: Is there any exemption for medical device manufacturing projects?
output: Yes, FDI up to 100% under the automatic route is permitted for manufacturing of medical devices and the conditions mentioned above will not be applicable to these projects.

input: How often should banks exchange information about the conduct of borrowers' accounts with other banks?
output: Banks should exchange information at least quarterly.

input: How should the statement pertaining to March Residual Transactions be marked?
output: The statement pertaining to March Residual Transactions should be clearly marked as 'March Residual Account'.

input: What is the deadline for Indian companies to file the annual return on Foreign Liabilities and Assets?
output: The deadline for filing the annual return on Foreign Liabilities and Assets is July 15 of every year.

input: How should the information on cross border wire transfers be furnished to the Director of FIU-IND?
output: The information on cross border wire transfers should be furnished electronically in the FIN-Net module developed by FIU-IND. The 'Transaction Based Reporting Format' (TRF) already developed by FIU-IND for reporting Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs), Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs), and Non-Profit Organizations Transaction Reports (NTRs) can be used for reporting cross border wire transfers.

input: What steps should bank branches take to ensure accuracy in PAN and CIN details uploaded to OLTAS?
output: Bank branches should use bulk PAN verification facility from TIN-NSDL site, insist on proof of PAN where necessary, and ensure that the CIN given to the customer is the one uploaded to OLTAS.

input: What is the minimum and maximum tenor for repo transactions in corporate debt securities?
output: The minimum tenor is one day and the maximum tenor is one year.

input: What is the notified amount for the auction of '8.35 percent Government Stock 2022'?
output: The notified amount for the auction of '8.35 percent Government Stock 2022' is Rs 5,000 crore (nominal).

input: What is the definition of "loss asset" as per the amended Directions?
output: "Loss asset" includes financial assets including Security Receipts that are not realized within the total time frame specified in the plan for realization formulated by the Securitisation Company or Reconstruction Company, and are held by them.

input: Can a person transfer foreign contribution to another person?
output: Yes, a person can transfer foreign contribution to another person, provided that the other person is also registered and has been granted a certificate or obtained prior permission under the Act.

input: What changes are proposed in the RBI circular for micro-finance delivery?
output: The proposed changes include providing incentives to branches for financing SHGs, leaving the group dynamics of SHGs to themselves, and making the approach to micro-financing hassle-free and flexible.

input: Can the Government Stock be transferred?
output: Yes, the Government Stock can be transferred by endorsement and delivery.

input: How much is the fees admissible to statutory central auditors for each branch of the bank?
output: The fees admissible to statutory central auditors is fixed at `750/- per branch for all branches of the bank in existence as on the date of reference for the audit.

input: What is the purpose of the RBI circular on opening and maintenance of Rupee / Foreign Currency Vostro Accounts of Non-resident Exchange Houses?
output: The purpose of the RBI circular is to provide instructions for opening and maintaining Rupee / Foreign Currency Vostro Accounts of Non-resident Exchange Houses.

input: What is the Scheme for Non-competitive Bidding Facility in the Auctions of Government Securities?
output: The Scheme allows eligible individuals and institutions to bid for Government Securities up to 5% of the notified amount of sale.

input: How does the interest rate on FCNR(B) deposits compare to previous rates?
output: The interest rate on FCNR(B) deposits has increased from LIBOR / SWAP rates plus 25 basis points to LIBOR / SWAP rates plus 100 basis points for the respective currency / corresponding maturities.

input: Which banks are allowed to act as market makers for FIIs under the Foreign Exchange Management (Foreign Exchange Derivative Contracts) Regulations, 2000?
output: Only designated branches of AD Category I banks maintaining accounts of FIIs are allowed to act as market makers for FIIs for hedging their currency risk on the market value of their entire investment in equity and/or debt in India.

input: Is participation on a non-competitive basis open to all entities?
output: Yes, subject to the conditions mentioned, any person including firms, companies, corporate bodies, institutions, provident funds, trusts, and any other entity prescribed by RBI can participate.

input: What is the timeline for compliance with the RBI circular on Implementation of Section 51-A of UAPA, 1967 and updates of the UNSCR 1267(1999) / 1989(2011) Committee’s Al Qaida Sanctions List?
output: The RBI circular, issued on February 4, 2015, states that the updates of the UNSCR 1267(1999) / 1989(2011) Committee’s Al Qaida Sanctions List must be complied with immediately. The circular also mentions specific dates, i.e. November 16, 2021, February 18, 2022, May 02, 2022, and May 13, 2022, for the withdrawal of notifications related to this matter. Therefore, compliance must be ensured within this timeline.

input: What are the differences in the application of prudential guidelines for banks and NBFCs?
output: Banks are subject to income recognition, asset classification and provisioning norms, capital adequacy norms, single and group borrower limits, prudential limits on capital market exposures, classification and valuation norms for the investment portfolio, CRR/SLR requirements, accounting and disclosure norms, and supervisory reporting requirements. NBFCs-D are subject to similar norms as banks, except for CRR requirements and prudential limits on capital market exposures. However, the norms apply at a lesser rigor than those for banks. Capital adequacy norms, CRR/SLR requirements, single and group borrower limits, prudential limits on capital market exposures, and restrictions on investments in land and building and unquoted shares do not apply to NBFCs-ND.

input: Can bank branches refuse to accept small denomination notes and coins?
output: No, bank branches are not allowed to refuse acceptance of small denomination notes and coins tendered at their counters.

input: Who voted for and against the repo rate hike?
output: Dr. Shashanka Bhide, Dr. Ashima Goyal, Dr. Rajiv Ranjan, Dr. Michael Debabrata Patra and Shri Shaktikanta Das voted to increase the policy repo rate by 35 basis points. Prof. Jayanth R. Varma voted against the repo rate hike.

input: When will the results of the auction be announced?
output: The results of the auction will be announced on May 8, 2009, at the Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office.

input: When and where will the auction take place and what are the trading guidelines for the Stock?
output: The auction will be conducted by Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai Office, Fort, Mumbai-400 001 on August 6, 2010. Bids can be submitted in electronic format on the Negotiated Dealing System (NDS) on the same day. The Stock will be eligible for "When Issued" trading in accordance with the guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank of India.

input: What is the procedure for updating the list of individuals/entities linked to Al-Qaida?
output: Regulated Entities (REs) are required to update the list of individuals/entities as circulated by the RBI and ensure that no account is held by or linked to any of the entities or individuals included in the list before opening a new account.

input: How can RRBs relax the distance criteria for retail outlets/sub-agents of BCs?
output: RRBs can request relaxation of the distance criteria from the District Consultative Committee (DCC)/State level Bankers Committee (SLBC) on merit basis, especially in under-banked areas.

input: Are there any other changes in the instructions for NRE deposits?
output: No, there are no other changes in the instructions for NRE deposits. The other instructions as contained in the Master Circular DBOD.No.Dir.BC.5/13.03.00/2005-06 dated July 1, 2005, as amended from time to time, remain unchanged.

input: What is the consequence of violating the RBI Directions on preparation of Balance Sheet for NBFCs?
output: Violation of the RBI Directions may result in penalties or other regulatory action against the NBFC. Therefore, it is important for NBFCs to comply with the notification and seek prior approval from RBI for any extension of their financial year.

input: What was the announcement made by the Hon’ble Finance Minister in his Budget Speech for 2008-09?
output: The Hon’ble Finance Minister announced that short-term crop loans will continue to be disbursed at 7 per cent per annum and an initial provision of Rs.1,600 crore will be made for interest subvention in 2008-09.

input: What is the deadline for NBFCs to report their secondary market transactions in corporate bonds on FIMMDA's reporting platform?
output: NBFCs are required to report their secondary market transactions in corporate bonds on FIMMDA's reporting platform with effect from September 1, 2007.

input: What is the minimum CRAR prescribed by the RBI?
output: The minimum CRAR prescribed by the RBI is mentioned in the circular dated November 26, 2013.

input: What is the significance of the circular issued by RBI on Legal Guardianship Certificates?
output: The circular issued by RBI provides guidance to banks on the legal provisions of the Mental Health Act, 1987 and the acceptance of orders/certificates issued by the competent authority for appointing guardians/managers for bank account operations.

input: Is a Securitisation Company/Reconstruction Company considered a bank or financial institution under the SARFAESI Act, 2002?
output: No, a SC/RC is not considered a bank or financial institution under the SARFAESI Act, 2002.

input: What is the purpose of the Master Circular on External Commercial Borrowings and Trade Credits?
output: The purpose of the Master Circular is to consolidate all existing instructions on the subject of External Commercial Borrowings (ECB) and Trade Credits issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) into one document for easy reference.

input: Can Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) avail of ECBs for on-lending to the infrastructure sector?
